# lance

```go
import "github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
```

Package lance provides Go bindings for the Lance columnar format, implemented as cgo bindings over the lance\-go\-ffi Rust static library \(see rust/ and include/lance\_go.h in this repository\).

Build the native library first with \`make rust\` from the repository root.

Handles \(Dataset, Session, ...\) wrap native resources and must be closed with Close; each constructor also registers a runtime.AddCleanup leak safety net, but that is only a backstop, not a substitute for calling Close. Every method that takes a context.Context cancels its in\-flight native call when the context is done, surfacing context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded. Arrow data passed into this package \(Write, MergeInsert, query vectors, ...\) crosses the Arrow C Data Interface, so its buffers must live outside the Go heap: build it with Allocator, never memory.DefaultAllocator, or it crashes under GOEXPERIMENT=cgocheck2. Errors wrap exactly one sentinel from errors.go \(ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidArgument, ...\) so callers can classify failures with errors.Is. See docs/ in this repository for narrative guides.

## Constants

<a name="CompressionMetaKey"></a>Arrow field\-metadata keys understood by the Lance encoder. Set them on a column's arrow.Field before Write to control on\-disk encoding. Values must match the exact strings the Lance encoder recognizes \(see the Compression\* and StructuralEncoding\* constants\). These mirror lance\-encoding/src/constants.rs and lance\-arrow BLOB\_META\_KEY.

```go
const (
    // CompressionMetaKey selects the column compression codec (one of the
    // Compression* constants).
    CompressionMetaKey = "lance-encoding:compression"
    // CompressionLevelMetaKey sets the codec compression level (integer).
    CompressionLevelMetaKey = "lance-encoding:compression-level"
    // RLEThresholdMetaKey sets the run-length-encoding threshold.
    RLEThresholdMetaKey = "lance-encoding:rle-threshold"
    // StructuralEncodingMetaKey selects the structural encoding (one of the
    // StructuralEncoding* constants).
    StructuralEncodingMetaKey = "lance-encoding:structural-encoding"
    // PackedMetaKey requests packed struct encoding when set to "true".
    PackedMetaKey = "lance-encoding:packed"
    // BSSMetaKey controls byte-stream-split (one of the BSS* constants).
    BSSMetaKey = "lance-encoding:bss"
    // DictDivisorMetaKey, DictSizeRatioMetaKey,
    // DictValuesCompressionMetaKey and DictValuesCompressionLevelMetaKey tune
    // dictionary encoding.
    DictDivisorMetaKey                = "lance-encoding:dict-divisor"
    DictSizeRatioMetaKey              = "lance-encoding:dict-size-ratio"
    DictValuesCompressionMetaKey      = "lance-encoding:dict-values-compression"
    DictValuesCompressionLevelMetaKey = "lance-encoding:dict-values-compression-level"
    // BlobMetaKey marks a large-binary column as a blob column when set to
    // "true" (matches lance-arrow BLOB_META_KEY).
    BlobMetaKey = "lance-encoding:blob"
)
```

<a name="CompressionNone"></a>Column compression codecs \(values for CompressionMetaKey\).

```go
const (
    // CompressionNone disables compression for the column.
    CompressionNone = "none"
    // CompressionLZ4 selects the LZ4 codec.
    CompressionLZ4 = "lz4"
    // CompressionZstd selects the Zstandard codec.
    CompressionZstd = "zstd"
    // CompressionFsst selects the FSST codec (string-oriented compression).
    CompressionFsst = "fsst"
)
```

<a name="StructuralEncodingMiniblock"></a>Structural encodings \(values for StructuralEncodingMetaKey\).

```go
const (
    // StructuralEncodingMiniblock selects the miniblock structural encoding.
    StructuralEncodingMiniblock = "miniblock"
    // StructuralEncodingFullzip selects the fullzip structural encoding.
    StructuralEncodingFullzip = "fullzip"
)
```

<a name="BSSOff"></a>Byte\-stream\-split modes \(values for BSSMetaKey\).

```go
const (
    // BSSOff disables byte-stream-split.
    BSSOff = "off"
    // BSSOn forces byte-stream-split.
    BSSOn = "on"
    // BSSAuto lets the encoder decide whether to apply byte-stream-split.
    BSSAuto = "auto"
)
```

<a name="AggCount"></a>Aggregate function names accepted by AggFunc.Func.

```go
const (
    // AggCount counts rows (or a column's non-NULL values).
    AggCount = "count"
    // AggSum sums a column's values.
    AggSum = "sum"
    // AggAvg averages a column's values.
    AggAvg = "avg"
    // AggMin takes the minimum of a column's values.
    AggMin = "min"
    // AggMax takes the maximum of a column's values.
    AggMax = "max"
)
```

<a name="MaterializationHeuristic"></a>Materialization styles accepted by Scanner.MaterializationStyle.

```go
const (
    // MaterializationHeuristic picks early/late per column based on column
    // size and storage type (the default).
    MaterializationHeuristic = "heuristic"
    // MaterializationAllLate fetches filtered columns only for matching rows.
    MaterializationAllLate = "all_late"
    // MaterializationAllEarly fetches whole columns before filtering.
    MaterializationAllEarly = "all_early"
)
```

<a name="ApproxModeFast"></a>Approximation modes accepted by Scanner.ApproxMode.

```go
const (
    // ApproxModeFast prefers lower latency over recall.
    ApproxModeFast = "fast"
    // ApproxModeNormal is the default latency/recall balance.
    ApproxModeNormal = "normal"
    // ApproxModeAccurate prefers recall over latency.
    ApproxModeAccurate = "accurate"
)
```

<a name="BlobsAllBinary"></a>Blob handling modes accepted by Scanner.BlobHandling.

```go
const (
    // BlobsAllBinary reads all blob columns as binary data.
    BlobsAllBinary = "all_binary"
    // BlobsDescriptions reads blob columns as descriptions and other binary
    // columns as binary (the default).
    BlobsDescriptions = "blobs_descriptions"
    // BlobsAllDescriptions reads all binary columns as descriptions.
    BlobsAllDescriptions = "all_descriptions"
)
```

<a name="FuzzinessAuto"></a>FuzzinessAuto selects the edit distance automatically from each term's length \(0 for length \<= 2, 1 for length \<= 5, 2 above\).

```go
const FuzzinessAuto = -1
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrInvalidArgument"></a>Sentinel errors mirroring the ErrorCode enum in rust/src/error.rs. Errors returned by this package wrap exactly one of these, so callers can classify failures with errors.Is.

Sentinels carry NO "lance: " prefix. The single package prefix is added exactly once at the wrap layer \(the datasetOp/datasetDo/fragmentOp helpers in ops.go, or the hand\-rolled "lance: \<verb\>: %w" wraps\), so error strings read "lance: \<verb\>: \<sentinel\>\[: \<native message\>\]".

```go
var (
    // ErrInvalidArgument is returned when a call receives a malformed or
    // out-of-range argument (e.g. an unresolvable Ref, a negative offset).
    ErrInvalidArgument = errors.New("invalid argument")
    // ErrIO is returned when the underlying object store or filesystem
    // reports a read/write failure.
    ErrIO = errors.New("io error")
    // ErrNotFound is returned when the referenced dataset, version, tag,
    // branch, fragment, or file does not exist.
    ErrNotFound = errors.New("not found")
    // ErrAlreadyExists is returned when a create-only operation targets a
    // name or path that is already taken (e.g. an existing tag or branch).
    ErrAlreadyExists = errors.New("already exists")
    // ErrIndex is returned when an index build, load, or query fails.
    ErrIndex = errors.New("index error")
    // ErrInternal is returned for native-side failures that do not map to a
    // more specific sentinel, including panics caught at the FFI boundary.
    ErrInternal = errors.New("internal error")
    // ErrNotImplemented is returned when the requested operation or option
    // combination is not supported by the underlying Lance crate.
    ErrNotImplemented = errors.New("not implemented")
    // ErrConflict is returned when a commit loses a concurrent-write race
    // against another writer (retry against the latest version).
    ErrConflict = errors.New("conflict")
    // ErrTimeout is returned when the native side reports its own timeout,
    // distinct from ctx exceeding its deadline (which surfaces as
    // context.DeadlineExceeded).
    ErrTimeout = errors.New("timeout")
    // ErrReentrantCall is returned when a synchronous Go callback (an
    // AddColumnsUDF mapper, a UDFCheckpointStore method, or a WriteWithProgress
    // callback) re-enters this package while the native operation that invoked
    // it is still running. Such a nested call would drive the shared tokio
    // runtime from within itself, which would panic in Tokio, so it is rejected
    // up front. Callbacks must not call
    // back into lance-go. See the docs on those APIs.
    ErrReentrantCall = errors.New("reentrant call from within a callback")
    // ErrCanceled is the defensive fallback sentinel for ERROR_CODE_CANCELLED.
    // In practice a cancelled FFI call surfaces ctx.Err() directly
    // (context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded); ErrCanceled is only
    // returned in the theoretical race where the native side reports
    // cancellation before the Go context observes it.
    ErrCanceled = errors.New("operation canceled")
)
```

<a name="Allocator"></a>
## func [Allocator](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/allocator.go#L13>)

```go
func Allocator() memory.Allocator
```

Allocator returns a C\-backed Arrow allocator suitable for every record or array that may cross into Lance through cgo. Prefer this helper to Arrow's memory.DefaultAllocator for Write, Merge, Append, UDF output, and query vectors; Go\-heap\-backed Arrow buffers cannot safely cross the C Data Interface under cgocheck2.

<a name="MarkBlobColumn"></a>
## func [MarkBlobColumn](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L121>)

```go
func MarkBlobColumn(field arrow.Field) arrow.Field
```

MarkBlobColumn returns field marked as a Lance blob column. Use on a large\_binary column to store its values as external blobs.

<a name="Records"></a>
## func [Records](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/records.go#L27>)

```go
func Records(schema *arrow.Schema, batches ...arrow.RecordBatch) (array.RecordReader, error)
```

Records returns an array.RecordReader over the given batches, all of which must match schema. It is a thin, validated wrapper over array.NewRecordReader: schema must not be nil, and every batch's schema must equal schema exactly, or the offending batch's index is named in the returned error. Zero batches is allowed and yields a reader that is immediately exhausted \(Next returns false, Err returns nil\); passing that reader to Write succeeds and produces a valid, zero\-row dataset.

Per array.NewRecordReader's semantics \(arrow\-go v18\), the returned reader takes its own reference on each batch \(it calls Retain on every batch before returning\). Callers keep their own reference to each batch and may Release it immediately after this call returns; the reader remains valid until it is itself Released, at which point it releases its own references in turn. This call does not change where the underlying buffers must live: data destined for lance.Write \(or any other entry point that crosses the Arrow C Data Interface\) must still be allocated with Allocator, not memory.DefaultAllocator, regardless of how it is wrapped here.

<a name="SetFieldCompression"></a>
## func [SetFieldCompression](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L105>)

```go
func SetFieldCompression(field arrow.Field, codec string, opts ...CompressionOption) arrow.Field
```

SetFieldCompression returns field with the compression codec \(a Compression\* constant\) and any options applied as Arrow metadata. Existing metadata is preserved.

<a name="SetFieldMetadata"></a>
## func [SetFieldMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L88>)

```go
func SetFieldMetadata(field arrow.Field, kv map[string]string) arrow.Field
```

SetFieldMetadata returns field with kv merged into its Arrow metadata \(existing keys are overwritten, others preserved\).

<a name="SetFieldStructuralEncoding"></a>
## func [SetFieldStructuralEncoding](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L115>)

```go
func SetFieldStructuralEncoding(field arrow.Field, encoding string) arrow.Field
```

SetFieldStructuralEncoding returns field with the structural encoding \(a StructuralEncoding\* constant\) applied.

<a name="Version"></a>
## func [Version](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/lance.go#L34>)

```go
func Version() string
```

Version reports the version of the underlying lance\-go\-ffi native library and the Lance crate it was built against, e.g. "lance\-go\-ffi \<version\> \(lance 8.0.0\)".

<a name="AggFunc"></a>
## type [AggFunc](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L237-L246>)

AggFunc is one aggregate function application within an AggSpec.

```go
type AggFunc struct {
    // Func is one of AggCount, AggSum, AggAvg, AggMin, AggMax.
    Func string `json:"func"`
    // Column is the column to aggregate. For AggCount an empty Column (or
    // "*") counts all rows. A named column counts its non-NULL values.
    Column string `json:"column,omitempty"`
    // Alias renames the output column (default: the DataFusion name, e.g.
    // "count(1)" or "sum(score)").
    Alias string `json:"alias,omitempty"`
}
```

<a name="AggSpec"></a>
## type [AggSpec](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L251-L256>)

AggSpec configures Scanner.Aggregate: the scan result is replaced by one row per GroupBy group \(a single row when GroupBy is empty\), with one column per GroupBy column followed by one column per aggregate.

```go
type AggSpec struct {
    // GroupBy lists the columns to group by (may be empty).
    GroupBy []string `json:"group_by,omitempty"`
    // Aggregates lists the aggregate functions to compute.
    Aggregates []AggFunc `json:"aggregates"`
}
```

<a name="BTree"></a>
## type [BTree](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L79-L83>)

BTree configures a BTree index, suited to high\-cardinality scalar columns.

```go
type BTree struct {
    // ZoneSize is the number of rows per BTree zone (0 = Lance default).
    ZoneSize uint64
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BasePath"></a>
## type [BasePath](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L42-L54>)

BasePath registers an additional storage location \(bucket/directory\) in a dataset manifest, for multi\-base datasets.

```go
type BasePath struct {
    // ID uniquely identifies the base within the dataset, must be non-zero.
    ID  uint32 `json:"id"`
    // Name is an optional human-readable name the base can be referenced
    // by (e.g. in WithTargetBaseNamesOrPaths).
    Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
    // IsDatasetRoot marks the base holding the dataset root (as opposed to
    // a data-only base).
    IsDatasetRoot bool `json:"is_dataset_root,omitempty"`
    // Path is the full URI of the base, e.g. "s3://bucket/path" or a local
    // directory.
    Path string `json:"path"`
}
```

<a name="Bitmap"></a>
## type [Bitmap](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L92>)

Bitmap configures a Bitmap index, suited to low\-cardinality columns.

```go
type Bitmap struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BlobFile"></a>
## type [BlobFile](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L57-L60>)

BlobFile is a handle to one blob's bytes, obtained from BlobList.Get. It is borrowed from its BlobList and becomes invalid once the list is closed.

```go
type BlobFile struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BlobFile.Close"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Close](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L424>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Close() error
```

Close closes the underlying blob file early, releasing its I/O resources. The handle memory is still owned by the BlobList, and further reads fail after Close.

<a name="BlobFile.Read"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Read](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L213>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Read(ctx context.Context) (res []byte, err error)
```

Read reads the blob from its current cursor to the end and returns the bytes, advancing the cursor. On a freshly taken blob \(cursor 0\) this returns the whole blob. After Seek\(off\) it returns the bytes from off onward.

<a name="BlobFile.ReadAll"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [ReadAll](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L229>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) ReadAll(ctx context.Context) ([]byte, error)
```

ReadAll reads from the blob's current cursor to the end. It is the io.ReadAll\-style name for Read; Read remains available for compatibility.

<a name="BlobFile.ReadRange"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [ReadRange](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L333>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) ReadRange(ctx context.Context, start, length uint64) (res []byte, err error)
```

ReadRange reads length bytes starting at blob\-local offset start \(random access, it does not move the cursor\).

<a name="BlobFile.Reader"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Reader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L237>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Reader(ctx context.Context) *BlobReader
```

Reader returns a context\-bound, independently positioned reader for this blob. The reader implements io.Reader, io.ReaderAt, and io.Seeker and fetches only the requested ranges, avoiding whole\-blob materialization. It remains borrowed from the BlobList and becomes invalid when the list is closed.

<a name="BlobFile.Seek"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Seek](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L368>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Seek(ctx context.Context, off uint64) (err error)
```

Seek moves the blob's read cursor to off.

<a name="BlobFile.Size"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Size](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L349>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Size() uint64
```

Size returns the blob's total size in bytes. It returns 0 if the list is closed.

<a name="BlobFile.Tell"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [Tell](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L382>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) Tell(ctx context.Context) (n uint64, err error)
```

Tell returns the blob's current cursor position.

<a name="BlobFile.URI"></a>
### func \(\*BlobFile\) [URI](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L400>)

```go
func (b *BlobFile) URI(ctx context.Context) (res string, err error)
```

URI returns the blob's URI, or an empty string when it has none.

<a name="BlobList"></a>
## type [BlobList](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L27-L38>)

BlobList is a handle to a set of blob files returned by Dataset.TakeBlobs \(and its ByIndices/ByAddresses variants\). It owns the blob files, and every BlobFile obtained from Get is only valid until the BlobList is closed.

A BlobList is self\-contained: its blob reads stay valid after the originating Dataset is closed. Close must be called to release native resources. It is idempotent and safe for concurrent use.

```go
type BlobList struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BlobList.Close"></a>
### func \(\*BlobList\) [Close](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L160>)

```go
func (l *BlobList) Close() error
```

Close releases the blob list and every blob file it owns. It is idempotent. BlobFiles obtained from Get must not be used after Close. Close waits for any in\-flight blob reads to finish \(it takes the write lock\).

<a name="BlobList.Get"></a>
### func \(\*BlobList\) [Get](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L144>)

```go
func (l *BlobList) Get(i int) *BlobFile
```

Get returns a handle to the i\-th blob file. It returns nil if the list is closed or i is out of range. The returned BlobFile is valid until the BlobList is closed.

<a name="BlobList.Len"></a>
### func \(\*BlobList\) [Len](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L128>)

```go
func (l *BlobList) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of blob files in the list.

<a name="BlobReader"></a>
## type [BlobReader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L246-L251>)

BlobReader is a chunked reader over a BlobFile. Its sequential cursor is independent from BlobFile.Seek/Tell and from other BlobReaders.

```go
type BlobReader struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BlobReader.Read"></a>
### func \(\*BlobReader\) [Read](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L254>)

```go
func (r *BlobReader) Read(p []byte) (int, error)
```

Read implements io.Reader.

<a name="BlobReader.ReadAt"></a>
### func \(\*BlobReader\) [ReadAt](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L263>)

```go
func (r *BlobReader) ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (int, error)
```

ReadAt implements io.ReaderAt without changing the sequential cursor.

<a name="BlobReader.Seek"></a>
### func \(\*BlobReader\) [Seek](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L300>)

```go
func (r *BlobReader) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error)
```

Seek implements io.Seeker for the sequential cursor.

<a name="BloomFilter"></a>
## type [BloomFilter](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L123-L131>)

BloomFilter configures a BloomFilter index.

```go
type BloomFilter struct {
    // NumberOfItems is the expected number of distinct items the filter is
    // sized for (0 = Lance default, 8192).
    NumberOfItems uint64
    // Probability is the target false-positive rate (0 = Lance default,
    // 0.00057).
    Probability float64
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BooleanQuery"></a>
## type [BooleanQuery](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L980-L984>)

BooleanQuery combines sub\-queries: rows must match all Must queries, must not match any MustNot query, and Should queries raise the score \(at least one of Should/Must must be non\-empty\).

```go
type BooleanQuery struct {
    Should  []FtsQuery
    Must    []FtsQuery
    MustNot []FtsQuery
}
```

<a name="BoostQuery"></a>
## type [BoostQuery](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L912-L919>)

BoostQuery scores rows matching Positive higher and rows matching Negative lower.

```go
type BoostQuery struct {
    // Positive is the query whose matches are wanted.
    Positive FtsQuery
    // Negative is the query whose matches are penalized.
    Negative FtsQuery
    // NegativeBoost scales the negative contribution (default 0.5).
    NegativeBoost float32
}
```

<a name="BranchInfo"></a>
## type [BranchInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L30-L42>)

BranchInfo describes a branch of a dataset.

```go
type BranchInfo struct {
    // ParentBranch is the branch this one was created from, or "" for the
    // main branch.
    ParentBranch string `json:"parentBranch"`
    // ParentVersion is the version the branch was created at.
    ParentVersion uint64 `json:"parentVersion"`
    // CreatedAtUnix is the branch creation time as a Unix timestamp.
    CreatedAtUnix uint64 `json:"createAt"`
    // ManifestSize is the size in bytes of the branch head's manifest.
    ManifestSize uint64 `json:"manifestSize"`
    // Metadata holds key-value pairs attached to the branch.
    Metadata map[string]string `json:"metadata"`
}
```

<a name="CacheBackend"></a>
## type [CacheBackend](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/cache.go#L34-L47>)

CacheBackend is an external key/value store for serializable Lance index cache entries, plugged into a Session via NewSessionWithCacheBackend. Only codec\-bearing entries reach it \(already serialized to bytes\). Non\- serializable live objects never cross the boundary. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.

This is a building block: the examples/plugincache reference implementation shows an in\-memory backend, and the same interface backs a disk or Redis store with no changes to lance\-go.

```go
type CacheBackend interface {
    // Get returns the bytes stored under key, and whether they were found.
    Get(key CacheKey) (val []byte, found bool)
    // Put stores val under key. sizeHint is the value's size in bytes.
    Put(key CacheKey, val []byte, sizeHint int)
    // InvalidatePrefix drops every entry whose key prefix starts with prefix.
    InvalidatePrefix(prefix string)
    // Clear drops every entry.
    Clear()
    // Len returns the number of entries.
    Len() int
    // SizeBytes returns the total stored byte size.
    SizeBytes() int64
}
```

<a name="CacheCounts"></a>
## type [CacheCounts](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L61-L70>)

CacheCounts reports hit/miss and size counters for one cache.

```go
type CacheCounts struct {
    // Hits is the number of lookups served from the cache.
    Hits uint64 `json:"hits"`
    // Misses is the number of lookups not found in the cache.
    Misses uint64 `json:"misses"`
    // NumEntries is the number of entries currently cached.
    NumEntries uint64 `json:"num_entries"`
    // SizeBytes is the approximate memory held by the cache.
    SizeBytes uint64 `json:"size_bytes"`
}
```

<a name="CacheKey"></a>
## type [CacheKey](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/cache.go#L19-L23>)

CacheKey identifies an entry in the Lance index cache. Prefix scopes the entry \(typically a dataset/index location\), Key is the entry name within the prefix, and TypeName is the Rust type of the cached value \(a stable discriminator you can treat as opaque\).

```go
type CacheKey struct {
    Prefix   string
    Key      string
    TypeName string
}
```

<a name="CacheStats"></a>
## type [CacheStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L328-L335>)

CacheStats describes the dataset session's cache usage.

```go
type CacheStats struct {
    // CacheSizeBytes is the approximate memory held by the session caches.
    CacheSizeBytes uint64 `json:"cache_size_bytes"`
    // IndexCacheEntryCount is the number of entries in the index cache.
    IndexCacheEntryCount uint64 `json:"index_cache_entry_count"`
    // IndexCacheHitRate is the index cache hit ratio in [0, 1].
    IndexCacheHitRate float32 `json:"index_cache_hit_rate"`
}
```

<a name="CleanupOption"></a>
## type [CleanupOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L114>)

CleanupOption configures CleanupOldVersions.

```go
type CleanupOption func(*cleanupConfig)
```

<a name="WithDeleteUnverified"></a>
### func [WithDeleteUnverified](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L119>)

```go
func WithDeleteUnverified(deleteUnverified bool) CleanupOption
```

WithDeleteUnverified controls whether files not referenced by any manifest are deleted too. Such files may belong to an in\-progress transaction, so only enable this when no other write is running \(default false\).

<a name="WithErrorIfTaggedOldVersions"></a>
### func [WithErrorIfTaggedOldVersions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L126>)

```go
func WithErrorIfTaggedOldVersions(errorIfTagged bool) CleanupOption
```

WithErrorIfTaggedOldVersions controls whether the cleanup fails when an old version that would be removed is still referenced by a tag \(default true in the engine\). Set to false to silently keep tagged versions.

<a name="CleanupPolicy"></a>
## type [CleanupPolicy](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L380-L398>)

CleanupPolicy selects which old dataset versions CleanupWithPolicy removes. The zero value removes nothing besides unreferenced files. Set BeforeTimestamp and/or BeforeVersion to select versions.

```go
type CleanupPolicy struct {
    // BeforeTimestamp removes versions committed before this time (zero
    // means no time bound).
    BeforeTimestamp time.Time
    // BeforeVersion removes versions numbered below this version (zero
    // means no version bound).
    BeforeVersion uint64
    // DeleteUnverified also deletes recent unverified (possibly
    // in-progress) files. Only set this when no other writes are running.
    DeleteUnverified bool
    // ErrorIfTaggedOldVersions makes the cleanup fail if a tagged version
    // matches the policy. nil (the zero value) protects tagged versions,
    // the lance default. Set it to false to allow removing tagged versions.
    ErrorIfTaggedOldVersions *bool
    // CleanReferencedBranches also cleans up referenced branches.
    CleanReferencedBranches bool
    // DeleteRateLimit caps delete requests per second (0 = unlimited).
    DeleteRateLimit uint64
}
```

<a name="ColumnAlteration"></a>
## type [ColumnAlteration](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L31-L45>)

ColumnAlteration describes a change to one existing column: a rename, a nullability change, a type cast, or any combination. Zero\-valued fields are left unchanged.

```go
type ColumnAlteration struct {
    // Path is the (dot-separated) path of the column to alter. Required.
    Path string
    // Rename is the new name of the column, or "" to keep the name.
    Rename string
    // Nullable sets whether the column is nullable, and nil keeps the current
    // nullability.
    Nullable *bool
    // DataType casts the column to a new type, or "" to keep the type.
    // Supported values: "int32", "int64", "float32", "float64", "utf8",
    // "large_utf8", "binary", "bool", "date32", "date64", "timestamp_us",
    // "timestamp_ns". Casting rewrites the column data and drops any
    // indices covering it.
    DataType string
}
```

<a name="CommitBuilder"></a>
## type [CommitBuilder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L264-L267>)

CommitBuilder commits one or more Transactions to a dataset. Build it with NewCommit and finish with Execute or ExecuteBatch.

```go
type CommitBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCommit"></a>
### func [NewCommit](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L272>)

```go
func NewCommit(uri string, opts ...ObsOption) *CommitBuilder
```

NewCommit begins a commit against the dataset at uri. It uses the OTel global providers for instrumentation. Attach explicit providers by passing ObsOptions \(WithTracerProvider, WithMeterProvider, WithLoggerProvider\).

<a name="CommitBuilder.Detached"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [Detached](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L306>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) Detached() *CommitBuilder
```

Detached makes Execute commit a DETACHED version: one that is not part of the dataset's lineage, never appears in Versions/Transactions, and can never become the latest version \(see Dataset.Restore for the normal, attached rollback path\). Useful for staging changes or "secondary" datasets whose lineage is tracked elsewhere.

The full transaction — operation, UUID, tag, and transaction properties — is committed losslessly. Execute's returned Dataset handle is checked out at the detached version: its Version has the detached high bit set, its ManifestLocation points at the detached manifest, and ReadTransaction reads the detached transaction back. Detached commits require V2 manifest paths \(the default for new datasets\) and an existing dataset. Dataset.ListDetachedManifests enumerates detached versions later.

<a name="CommitBuilder.EnableV2ManifestPaths"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [EnableV2ManifestPaths](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L287>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) EnableV2ManifestPaths() *CommitBuilder
```

EnableV2ManifestPaths writes v2\-style manifest paths.

<a name="CommitBuilder.Execute"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [Execute](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L351>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) Execute(ctx context.Context, txn *Transaction) (ds *Dataset, err error)
```

Execute commits a single Transaction and returns a handle to the resulting dataset version.

Cancellation is ambiguous: the native commit performs cancellable work after the atomic manifest write, so a ctx.Err\(\) return does not guarantee the commit was not durably applied — treat it like a network timeout and check the dataset's version/state before retrying, or the retry may apply the transaction twice.

<a name="CommitBuilder.ExecuteBatch"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [ExecuteBatch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L384>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) ExecuteBatch(ctx context.Context, txns []*Transaction) (versions []uint64, err error)
```

ExecuteBatch commits a batch of append Transactions in a single commit. Lance merges compatible appends into one manifest, so the result is a single new version. The returned slice holds that version.

<a name="CommitBuilder.MaxRetries"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [MaxRetries](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L313>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) MaxRetries(n uint32) *CommitBuilder
```

MaxRetries bounds commit conflict retries.

<a name="CommitBuilder.SkipAutoCleanup"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [SkipAutoCleanup](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L319>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) SkipAutoCleanup() *CommitBuilder
```

SkipAutoCleanup disables automatic old\-version cleanup on commit.

<a name="CommitBuilder.UseStableRowIDs"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [UseStableRowIDs](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L280>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) UseStableRowIDs() *CommitBuilder
```

UseStableRowIDs enables stable row IDs on the committed version.

<a name="CommitBuilder.WithStorageFormat"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [WithStorageFormat](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L332>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) WithStorageFormat(format string) *CommitBuilder
```

WithStorageFormat sets the storage format \(e.g. "2.0", "stable"\).

<a name="CommitBuilder.WithStorageOptions"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [WithStorageOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L338>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) WithStorageOptions(opts map[string]string) *CommitBuilder
```

WithStorageOptions passes object\-store options for the commit.

<a name="CommitBuilder.WithTransactionProperties"></a>
### func \(\*CommitBuilder\) [WithTransactionProperties](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L326>)

```go
func (b *CommitBuilder) WithTransactionProperties(props map[string]string) *CommitBuilder
```

WithTransactionProperties attaches key/value properties to the commit.

<a name="CompactionMetrics"></a>
## type [CompactionMetrics](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L78-L87>)

CompactionMetrics reports what CompactFiles changed.

```go
type CompactionMetrics struct {
    // FragmentsRemoved is the number of fragments that were replaced.
    FragmentsRemoved uint64 `json:"fragments_removed"`
    // FragmentsAdded is the number of new fragments written.
    FragmentsAdded uint64 `json:"fragments_added"`
    // FilesRemoved is the number of files removed, including deletion files.
    FilesRemoved uint64 `json:"files_removed"`
    // FilesAdded is the number of new files written.
    FilesAdded uint64 `json:"files_added"`
}
```

<a name="CompactionOptions"></a>
## type [CompactionOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L20-L44>)

CompactionOptions configures CompactFiles. The zero value uses the engine's defaults \(1M rows per fragment, 1024 rows per group, materialize deletions above a 10% threshold\). Only explicitly set fields are sent to the engine.

```go
type CompactionOptions struct {
    // TargetRowsPerFragment is the target number of rows per data file.
    // Fragments with fewer rows are candidates for compaction. 0 means the
    // default of 1 million.
    TargetRowsPerFragment uint64
    // MaxRowsPerGroup caps the number of rows per row group in rewritten
    // files. 0 means the default.
    MaxRowsPerGroup uint64
    // MaxBytesPerFile caps the (approximate) size of rewritten data files.
    MaxBytesPerFile *uint64
    // MaterializeDeletions controls whether fragments with deleted rows are
    // rewritten without the deletions (default true).
    MaterializeDeletions *bool
    // MaterializeDeletionsThreshold is the fraction of deleted rows above
    // which deletions are materialized (default 0.1).
    MaterializeDeletionsThreshold *float32
    // NumThreads is the number of compaction tasks to run in parallel
    // (default: number of CPUs).
    NumThreads *uint
    // BatchSize is the batch size used when scanning input fragments.
    BatchSize *uint64
    // DeferIndexRemap defers index remapping to a later optimize pass
    // (default false).
    DeferIndexRemap *bool
}
```

<a name="CompactionPlan"></a>
## type [CompactionPlan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L374-L384>)

CompactionPlan is the result of PlanCompaction: a set of independent CompactionTasks, plus the dataset version they were planned against.

```go
type CompactionPlan struct {
    // Tasks is the set of independent compaction units the plan computed.
    Tasks []CompactionTask `json:"tasks"`
    // ReadVersion is the dataset version the plan was computed against.
    ReadVersion uint64 `json:"read_version"`

    // Raw is the plan's full JSON, an escape hatch that also carries
    // "options" (the effective options the planner used, echoing defaults
    // for anything opts left unset).
    Raw json.RawMessage `json:"-"`
}
```

<a name="CompactionPlan.UnmarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*CompactionPlan\) [UnmarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L387>)

```go
func (p *CompactionPlan) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error
```

UnmarshalJSON captures the plan's raw JSON alongside the typed fields.

<a name="CompactionTask"></a>
## type [CompactionTask](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L352-L355>)

CompactionTask is one group of fragments to compact, a standalone unit of work within a CompactionPlan. Its contents \(the lance TaskData: the fragment list to rewrite\) are kept opaque JSON: they mirror the engine's internal Fragment representation, which this package does not otherwise model. Ship Payload verbatim to a worker for round\-trip execution once a distributed\-execute FFI export exists \(see the package notes; execution and commit are not yet exposed\).

```go
type CompactionTask struct {
    // Payload is the task's opaque JSON contents.
    Payload json.RawMessage
}
```

<a name="CompactionTask.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(CompactionTask\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L365>)

```go
func (t CompactionTask) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalJSON re\-emits Payload verbatim \(round\-trip support for shipping a task to a worker and back\).

<a name="CompactionTask.UnmarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*CompactionTask\) [UnmarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L358>)

```go
func (t *CompactionTask) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error
```

UnmarshalJSON captures the task's raw JSON element verbatim.

<a name="CompressionOption"></a>
## type [CompressionOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L72>)

CompressionOption tunes SetFieldCompression.

```go
type CompressionOption func(map[string]string)
```

<a name="WithCompressionLevel"></a>
### func [WithCompressionLevel](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L75>)

```go
func WithCompressionLevel(level int) CompressionOption
```

WithCompressionLevel sets the codec compression level.

<a name="WithRLEThreshold"></a>
### func [WithRLEThreshold](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/encoding.go#L80>)

```go
func WithRLEThreshold(threshold float64) CompressionOption
```

WithRLEThreshold sets the run\-length\-encoding threshold.

<a name="DataFileInfo"></a>
## type [DataFileInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L38-L47>)

DataFileInfo describes one data file of a fragment \(see FragmentMetadata\).

```go
type DataFileInfo struct {
    // Path is the data file path relative to the dataset root.
    Path string `json:"path"`
    // Fields are the field ids stored in this file.
    Fields []int32 `json:"fields"`
    // FileMajorVersion is the Lance file format major version.
    FileMajorVersion uint32 `json:"file_major_version"`
    // FileMinorVersion is the Lance file format minor version.
    FileMinorVersion uint32 `json:"file_minor_version"`
}
```

<a name="DataStatistics"></a>
## type [DataStatistics](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L459-L462>)

DataStatistics describes the storage of the dataset's data.

```go
type DataStatistics struct {
    // Fields holds per-field statistics.
    Fields []FieldStatistics `json:"fields"`
}
```

<a name="Dataset"></a>
## type [Dataset](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L31-L46>)

Dataset is a handle to an opened Lance dataset. It is safe for concurrent use. Close must be called to release the native resources. It is idempotent.

```go
type Dataset struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="CommitOperation"></a>
### func [CommitOperation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L686>)

```go
func CommitOperation(ctx context.Context, uri string, op Operation, readVersion uint64, opts ...func(*CommitBuilder)) (ds *Dataset, err error)
```

CommitOperation builds op from JSON, wraps it in a fresh transaction at readVersion, commits it against uri, and returns the resulting dataset version. Use this for operations that can be constructed directly \(config updates, restores\). Richer operations travel losslessly via WriteFragments.

<a name="Open"></a>
### func [Open](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L325>)

```go
func Open(ctx context.Context, uri string, opts ...OpenOption) (ds *Dataset, err error)
```

Open opens an existing Lance dataset at uri.

Options compose freely. WithSession\(s\) shares s's index/metadata caches with the open, and WithObjectStoreCache\(c\) wraps the dataset's object store with a Go byte\-range cache. Passing both opens the dataset with a shared session AND a byte cache on the same handle.

Fails with ErrNotFound if uri does not exist, or if the requested version/tag/branch does not.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleOpen writes a small dataset and then reopens it by URI with Open, printing the field names of its schema.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-open-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "name", Type: arrow.BinaryTypes.String},
	}, nil)
	b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
	defer b.Release()
	b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder).Append(1)
	b.Field(1).(*array.StringBuilder).Append("alice")
	rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
	defer rec.Release()
	rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	rdr.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	ds.Close()

	opened, err := lance.Open(ctx, uri)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer opened.Close()

	openedSchema, err := opened.Schema(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	for _, f := range openedSchema.Fields() {
		fmt.Println(f.Name)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
id
name
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OpenWithObjectStoreCache"></a>
### func [OpenWithObjectStoreCache](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/cache.go#L166>)

```go
func OpenWithObjectStoreCache(ctx context.Context, uri string, cache ObjectStoreCache, opts ...OpenOption) (*Dataset, error)
```

OpenWithObjectStoreCache opens the dataset at uri \(with the given OpenOptions\) and wraps its object store so that immutable file byte\-range reads are served through cache. The returned Dataset behaves like any other. Its Close releases the native handle; native children retain the cache registration until they are released. It is a thin wrapper over Open\(ctx, uri, append\(opts, WithObjectStoreCache\(cache\)\)...\).

<a name="Write"></a>
### func [Write](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L413>)

```go
func Write(ctx context.Context, uri string, rdr array.RecordReader, opts ...WriteOption) (ds *Dataset, err error)
```

Write writes all record batches from rdr to a Lance dataset at uri and returns a handle to the resulting dataset. The reader is retained for the duration of the write and fully consumed. The caller keeps its own reference and should still Release it as usual.

The reader's batches are exported across the Arrow C Data Interface, so their buffers must live outside the Go heap to satisfy the cgo pointer\-passing rules: allocate them with Allocator \(this is enforced when building with GOEXPERIMENT=cgocheck2\).

Fails with ErrAlreadyExists if a dataset already exists at uri under the default WriteModeCreate; pass WithMode\(WriteModeAppend\) or WithMode\(WriteModeOverwrite\) instead.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleWrite creates a new dataset from an Arrow record reader and reports how many rows were written. Records exported across the Arrow C Data Interface must live outside the Go heap, so the builder uses lance.Allocator \(a C\-backed allocator\) rather than the Arrow default.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-write-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "name", Type: arrow.BinaryTypes.String},
	}, nil)
	b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
	defer b.Release()
	for i, name := range []string{"alice", "bob", "carol"} {
		b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder).Append(int64(i))
		b.Field(1).(*array.StringBuilder).Append(name)
	}
	rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
	defer rec.Release()
	rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer rdr.Release()

	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer ds.Close()

	count, err := ds.CountRows(ctx, "")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Println(count)
}
```

#### Output

```
3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="WriteWithProgress"></a>
### func [WriteWithProgress](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L216>)

```go
func WriteWithProgress(ctx context.Context, uri string, rdr array.RecordReader, progress func(WriteStats), opts ...WriteOption) (*Dataset, error)
```

WriteWithProgress writes rdr to a dataset at uri, reporting cumulative WriteStats to progress after each batch is written. It is a thin wrapper over Write with WithWriteProgress. The reader's batches must be C\-allocated \(see Write\).

progress runs synchronously on the write path and MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(opening/scanning a dataset, CountRows, etc.\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall rather than crashing the process. Because progress is best\-effort, that error is ignored and the write still completes.

<a name="Dataset.AddBases"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AddBases](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L160>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AddBases(ctx context.Context, bases []BasePath, properties map[string]string) error
```

AddBases registers additional storage base paths in the dataset manifest, committing a new version. The handle moves to that version. The bases can then be targeted by writes \(WithTargetBases\). properties, if non\-nil, is attached to the transaction.

<a name="Dataset.AddColumnsAllNulls"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AddColumnsAllNulls](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L133>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AddColumnsAllNulls(ctx context.Context, schema *arrow.Schema) error
```

AddColumnsAllNulls adds new all\-null columns described by schema, committing a new version. This is a metadata\-only operation. All fields in schema must be nullable.

<a name="Dataset.AddColumnsFromReader"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AddColumnsFromReader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L122>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AddColumnsFromReader(ctx context.Context, rdr array.RecordReader, batchSize uint32) error
```

AddColumnsFromReader adds new columns whose values are supplied by rdr, which must produce exactly one value per existing row, in order. The reader's batches cross the Arrow C Data Interface, so their buffers must live outside the Go heap \(see Write\). batchSize is currently unused by the engine for this transform. Pass 0.

<a name="Dataset.AddColumnsSQL"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AddColumnsSQL](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L102>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AddColumnsSQL(ctx context.Context, exprs []NamedExpr, readColumns []string, batchSize uint32) error
```

AddColumnsSQL adds new columns computed by SQL expressions over the existing rows, committing a new version. readColumns optionally restricts which existing columns are read to evaluate the expressions \(nil reads whatever the expressions reference\), and batchSize optionally overrides the scan batch size \(0 uses the default\).

<a name="Dataset.AddColumnsUDF"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AddColumnsUDF](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L137>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AddColumnsUDF(ctx context.Context, outSchema *arrow.Schema, fn func(in arrow.RecordBatch) (arrow.RecordBatch, error), opts ...UDFOption) error
```

AddColumnsUDF adds new columns whose values are computed by fn, one input RecordBatch at a time, committing a new version. outSchema is the schema of the columns fn returns, and fn's output must match it row\-for\-row with the input batch.

The result batch fn returns is exported across the Arrow C Data Interface, so its buffers must live outside the Go heap: build it with a C\-backed allocator returned by Allocator, as required under GOEXPERIMENT=cgocheck2. The input batch fn receives is valid only during the call.

fn runs synchronously inside the native add\-columns operation and MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(for example, opening or scanning a dataset, or calling CountRows\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall rather than crashing the process, and that error propagates out of AddColumnsUDF.

<a name="Dataset.AlterColumns"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [AlterColumns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L175>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) AlterColumns(ctx context.Context, alts ...ColumnAlteration) error
```

AlterColumns renames columns, changes their nullability and/or casts them to new types, committing a new version. Renames and nullability changes are zero\-copy. Type casts rewrite the column data and drop any indices covering it.

<a name="Dataset.Append"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Append](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L180>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Append(ctx context.Context, rdr array.RecordReader) (err error)
```

Append appends all record batches from rdr to the dataset this handle is checked out on, committing a new version and advancing the handle to it. When the handle is on a branch \(e.g. from CheckoutBranch\), the append commits to that branch.

The reader's batches are exported across the Arrow C Data Interface, so their buffers must live outside the Go heap \(allocate with a C\-backed allocator returned by Allocator, enforced under GOEXPERIMENT=cgocheck2\).

<a name="Dataset.CacheStats"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CacheStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L338>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CacheStats(ctx context.Context) (CacheStats, error)
```

CacheStats returns cache statistics of the dataset's session.

<a name="Dataset.Checkout"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Checkout](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L74>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Checkout(ctx context.Context, ref Ref) (*Dataset, error)
```

Checkout returns a NEW Dataset handle fixed at the referenced version. The receiver is left unchanged and stays usable. The caller must Close both handles independently. Fails with ErrNotFound if the version or tag does not exist.

<a name="Dataset.CheckoutBranch"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CheckoutBranch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L82>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CheckoutBranch(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Dataset, error)
```

CheckoutBranch returns a NEW Dataset handle checked out at the latest version of the named branch. The receiver is left unchanged. The caller must Close both handles independently.

<a name="Dataset.CheckoutLatest"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CheckoutLatest](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L100>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CheckoutLatest(ctx context.Context) error
```

CheckoutLatest moves this handle to the latest committed version of the dataset \(e.g. after another handle committed new versions or restored an old one\).

<a name="Dataset.CleanupOldVersions"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CleanupOldVersions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L160>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CleanupOldVersions(ctx context.Context, olderThan time.Duration, opts ...CleanupOption) (RemovalStats, error)
```

CleanupOldVersions removes versions older than olderThan \(and files unique to them\) from storage. Removed versions can no longer be checked out or restored. The current version is never removed.

<a name="Dataset.CleanupWithPolicy"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CleanupWithPolicy](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L415>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CleanupWithPolicy(ctx context.Context, policy CleanupPolicy) (RemovalStats, error)
```

CleanupWithPolicy removes old dataset versions \(and files unique to them\) according to policy, returning removal statistics. Removed versions can no longer be checked out or restored. For the common age\-based cleanup see CleanupOldVersions.

<a name="Dataset.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Close](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L497>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Close() error
```

Close releases the native dataset handle. It is idempotent and safe to call concurrently with other methods \(which will fail cleanly once the handle is closed\). Readers previously obtained from Scan remain valid.

<a name="Dataset.CommitIndexSegments"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CommitIndexSegments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L261>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CommitIndexSegments(ctx context.Context, name, column string, segments []*IndexMetadata) error
```

CommitIndexSegments commits one or more physical index segments as a single logical index named name over column, advancing the dataset handle to the new version.

<a name="Dataset.CompactFiles"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CompactFiles](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/maintenance.go#L133>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CompactFiles(ctx context.Context, opts CompactionOptions) (CompactionMetrics, error)
```

CompactFiles compacts small data files into larger ones and commits the result as a new version of the dataset. The handle is left at the new version.

<a name="Dataset.Config"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Config](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L79>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Config(ctx context.Context) (map[string]string, error)
```

Config returns the dataset configuration key\-value map from the manifest of the checked\-out version.

<a name="Dataset.CountDeletedRows"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CountDeletedRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L237>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CountDeletedRows(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
```

CountDeletedRows returns the number of soft\-deleted rows still present in the dataset's files \(reclaimable by compaction\).

<a name="Dataset.CountRows"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CountRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L524>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CountRows(ctx context.Context, filter string) (uint64, error)
```

CountRows returns the number of rows in the dataset. A non\-empty filter restricts the count to rows matching the SQL predicate. An empty filter counts all rows.

<a name="Dataset.CreateBranch"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CreateBranch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L58>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CreateBranch(ctx context.Context, name string, ref Ref) (*Dataset, error)
```

CreateBranch creates a branch named name pointing at the referenced version and returns a NEW Dataset handle checked out on the branch. The receiver stays on its current branch. The caller must Close both handles independently.

<a name="Dataset.CreateIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CreateIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L699>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CreateIndex(ctx context.Context, column string, cfg IndexConfig, opts ...IndexOption) error
```

CreateIndex builds an index of the configured type on column. Upon success a new dataset version is committed and the handle tracks it.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleDataset\_CreateIndex builds an IVF\_PQ vector index over 1024 rows of an 8\-dimensional vector column and runs an exact nearest\-neighbor lookup against it.

Row id's vector is a scaled one\-hot vector: all zero except at index id%8, which holds 1\+id\*0.001. Vectors sharing that basis index are only ever a few thousandths apart, while vectors on different basis indices are always more than 1.0 apart, so querying with the exact vector of a given id \(distance 0 to itself, and to no other row\) always yields that id as the unambiguous nearest match. Nprobes matches the partition count and Refine covers the whole dataset, so the search result is exact despite PQ quantization.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-create-index-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	const (
		dim  = 8
		rows = 1024
	)
	vecOf := func(id int64) []float32 {
		v := make([]float32, dim)
		v[id%dim] = 1 + float32(id)*0.001
		return v
	}

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "vec", Type: arrow.FixedSizeListOf(dim, arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Float32)},
	}, nil)
	b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
	defer b.Release()
	idB := b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder)
	vecB := b.Field(1).(*array.FixedSizeListBuilder)
	valB := vecB.ValueBuilder().(*array.Float32Builder)
	for id := int64(0); id < rows; id++ {
		idB.Append(id)
		vecB.Append(true)
		valB.AppendValues(vecOf(id), nil)
	}
	rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
	defer rec.Release()
	rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	rdr.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer ds.Close()

	if err := ds.CreateIndex(ctx, "vec",
		lance.IvfPq{Partitions: 4, SubVectors: 4, Bits: 8},
		lance.WithIndexName("vec_idx")); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	res, err := ds.Scan().
		Nearest("vec", vecOf(500), 1).
		Nprobes(4).
		Refine(rows).
		Reader(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer res.Release()
	for res.Next() {
		batch := res.RecordBatch()
		ids := batch.Column(batch.Schema().FieldIndices("id")[0]).(*array.Int64)
		for i := 0; i < int(batch.NumRows()); i++ {
			fmt.Println(ids.Value(i))
		}
	}
	if err := res.Err(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
500
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Dataset.CreateIndexUncommitted"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [CreateIndexUncommitted](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L150>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) CreateIndexUncommitted(ctx context.Context, column string, cfg IndexConfig, opts ...UncommittedOption) (*IndexMetadata, error)
```

CreateIndexUncommitted builds an uncommitted index segment over an optional fragment subset and returns it without committing. This is the distributed index\-build primitive: a driver mints shared centroids \(pass them via the IndexConfig's VectorOptions.Centroids with WithTrain\(false\)\), each worker builds a per\-fragment segment, then MergeIndexSegments \+ CommitIndexSegments finish the index.

<a name="Dataset.DataStats"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DataStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L465>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DataStats(ctx context.Context) (DataStatistics, error)
```

DataStats computes per\-field storage statistics for the dataset.

<a name="Dataset.DeepClone"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DeepClone](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L165>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DeepClone(ctx context.Context, targetURI string, ref Ref, storageOptions map[string]string) (*Dataset, error)
```

DeepClone clones the referenced version \(or the currently checked\-out version for the zero Ref\) into a fully independent dataset at targetURI, copying all data files. Fails if a dataset already exists at the target. Returns a handle to the clone, which the caller must Close.

<a name="Dataset.Delete"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Delete](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L73>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Delete(ctx context.Context, predicate string) (res DeleteResult, err error)
```

Delete removes all rows matching the SQL predicate and commits a new dataset version. The handle is updated in place to track the new version.

<a name="Dataset.DeleteBranch"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DeleteBranch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L100>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DeleteBranch(ctx context.Context, name string, force bool) error
```

DeleteBranch deletes the named branch. With force set, the branch's files are removed even if its metadata is missing \(zombie\-branch cleanup\).

<a name="Dataset.DeleteConfigKeys"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DeleteConfigKeys](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L119>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DeleteConfigKeys(ctx context.Context, keys ...string) error
```

DeleteConfigKeys removes keys from the dataset configuration, committing a new version, via the dedicated lance\_dataset\_delete\_config\_keys export. Missing keys are ignored.

<a name="Dataset.DeleteMetadataKeys"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DeleteMetadataKeys](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L163>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DeleteMetadataKeys(ctx context.Context, keys ...string) error
```

DeleteMetadataKeys removes keys from the table metadata, committing a new version. Missing keys are ignored.

<a name="Dataset.Delta"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Delta](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L56>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Delta() *DeltaBuilder
```

Delta starts building a change\-data\-capture query over the dataset. Tracking inserted/updated rows requires the dataset to be written with stable row ids \(WithStableRowIDs\(true\)\).

<a name="Dataset.DescribeIndices"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DescribeIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L952>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DescribeIndices(ctx context.Context, criteria *IndexCriteria) ([]IndexDescription, error)
```

DescribeIndices returns rich descriptions of the dataset's indices. When criteria is nil, every index is described. Otherwise only matching indices are returned. Unlike ListIndices, this does not load indices into memory but surfaces per\-type details, coverage and size.

<a name="Dataset.DropColumns"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DropColumns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L144>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DropColumns(ctx context.Context, cols ...string) error
```

DropColumns removes columns from the dataset, committing a new version. This is a metadata\-only operation. The column data is reclaimed later by CompactFiles and CleanupOldVersions.

<a name="Dataset.DropIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [DropIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L786>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) DropIndex(ctx context.Context, name string) error
```

DropIndex removes the index named name. Upon success a new dataset version is committed and the handle tracks it. Fails with ErrNotFound if no index named name exists.

<a name="Dataset.Fragment"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Fragment](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L125>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Fragment(ctx context.Context, id uint32) (*Fragment, error)
```

Fragment opens the fragment with the given id as a handle. It fails with ErrNotFound if no fragment has that id. The caller must Close the result.

<a name="Dataset.Fragments"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Fragments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L105>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Fragments(ctx context.Context) ([]FragmentInfo, error)
```

Fragments returns metadata for every fragment of the dataset. The fragments are returned in dataset order, and the sum of their NumRows equals the dataset's live row count.

<a name="Dataset.HasSuccessorVersion"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [HasSuccessorVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L269>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) HasSuccessorVersion(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
```

HasSuccessorVersion reports whether the immediate successor version's manifest exists. This is a fast contiguous\-history probe that may return false if intermediate manifests were cleaned up. Prefer IsStale for a general freshness check.

<a name="Dataset.IndexStatistics"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [IndexStatistics](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L767>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) IndexStatistics(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)
```

IndexStatistics returns a Lance\-defined JSON document describing the index named name \(type, number of indexed/unindexed rows, ...\).

<a name="Dataset.IsStale"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [IsStale](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L252>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) IsStale(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
```

IsStale reports whether the dataset has a newer committed version than the one checked out on this handle \(catch up with CheckoutLatest\).

<a name="Dataset.LatestVersion"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [LatestVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L582>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) LatestVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
```

LatestVersion returns the latest committed version number of the dataset, which may be newer than the checked\-out version when time traveling.

<a name="Dataset.ListBranches"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ListBranches](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L112>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ListBranches(ctx context.Context) (map[string]BranchInfo, error)
```

ListBranches returns all branches of the dataset, keyed by branch name.

<a name="Dataset.ListDetachedManifests"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ListDetachedManifests](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L614>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ListDetachedManifests(ctx context.Context) ([]DetachedManifest, error)
```

ListDetachedManifests returns the dataset's detached manifests.

<a name="Dataset.ListIndices"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ListIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L747>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ListIndices(ctx context.Context) ([]IndexInfo, error)
```

<a name="Dataset.LoadIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [LoadIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L1014>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) LoadIndex(ctx context.Context, uuid string) (res *IndexInfo, err error)
```

<a name="Dataset.LoadIndexByName"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [LoadIndexByName](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L1042>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) LoadIndexByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*IndexInfo, error)
```

LoadIndexByName returns the unique index with the given name, or nil if no such index exists. Fails wrapping ErrIndex if more than one index shares the name \(use LoadIndicesByName for delta indices\).

<a name="Dataset.LoadIndicesByName"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [LoadIndicesByName](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L1029>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) LoadIndicesByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (res []IndexInfo, err error)
```

<a name="Dataset.Manifest"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Manifest](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L586>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Manifest(ctx context.Context) (ManifestInfo, error)
```

<a name="Dataset.ManifestLocation"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ManifestLocation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L300>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ManifestLocation(ctx context.Context) (ManifestLocation, error)
```
