<a name="Dataset.Merge"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Merge](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L208>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Merge(ctx context.Context, rdr array.RecordReader, leftOn, rightOn string) error
```

Merge joins new columns onto the dataset, committing a new version: for each dataset row whose leftOn value matches a rightOn value in rdr, the remaining columns of rdr are appended to that row. Unmatched rows get nulls. The reader's batches cross the Arrow C Data Interface, so their buffers must live outside the Go heap \(see Write\).

<a name="Dataset.MergeIndexSegments"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [MergeIndexSegments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L222>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) MergeIndexSegments(ctx context.Context, segments []*IndexMetadata) (*IndexMetadata, error)
```

MergeIndexSegments merges uncommitted index segments into a single segment.

<a name="Dataset.MergeInsert"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [MergeInsert](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L206>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) MergeInsert(on ...string) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

MergeInsert starts building a merge\-insert of new source rows into the dataset, matching source and target rows on the given key columns.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleDataset\_MergeInsert upserts source rows into a dataset, matching on "id": rows present in the source update the matching target row, and unmatched source rows are inserted.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-merge-insert-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "value", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
	}, nil)

	// buildReader builds a one-batch reader over the given id/value pairs
	// using the C-backed Allocator, as required to cross the Arrow C Data
	// Interface into lance.Write / MergeInsertBuilder.Execute.
	buildReader := func(ids, values []int64) array.RecordReader {
		b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
		defer b.Release()
		b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder).AppendValues(ids, nil)
		b.Field(1).(*array.Int64Builder).AppendValues(values, nil)
		rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
		defer rec.Release()
		rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
		if err != nil {
			log.Fatal(err)
		}
		return rdr
	}

	rdr := buildReader([]int64{0, 1, 2}, []int64{10, 20, 30})
	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	rdr.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer ds.Close()

	before, err := ds.CountRows(ctx, "")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	// id=1 already exists (updated); id=3 does not (inserted).
	src := buildReader([]int64{1, 3}, []int64{200, 300})
	stats, err := ds.MergeInsert("id").
		WhenMatchedUpdateAll().
		WhenNotMatchedInsertAll().
		Execute(ctx, src)
	src.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	after, err := ds.CountRows(ctx, "")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("before=%d after=%d inserted=%d updated=%d\n",
		before, after, stats.NumInsertedRows, stats.NumUpdatedRows)
}
```

#### Output

```
before=3 after=4 inserted=1 updated=1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Dataset.Metadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Metadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L139>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Metadata(ctx context.Context) (map[string]string, error)
```

Metadata returns the table metadata key\-value map from the manifest of the checked\-out version.

<a name="Dataset.MigrateManifestPathsV2"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [MigrateManifestPathsV2](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L303>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) MigrateManifestPathsV2(ctx context.Context) error
```

MigrateManifestPathsV2 migrates the dataset to the V2 manifest naming scheme \(constant\-time latest\-manifest lookups on object storage\). The handle is re\-pointed at the migrated latest version. New datasets already use V2 paths unless written with WithV2ManifestPaths\(false\).

Unlike other operations, ctx cancellation does not abort the migration once it has started: it always runs to completion, because aborting mid\-migration would leave a mix of V1 and V2 manifest names that Lance refuses to open.

<a name="Dataset.NumSmallFiles"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [NumSmallFiles](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L314>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) NumSmallFiles(ctx context.Context, maxRowsPerGroup uint64) (uint64, error)
```

NumSmallFiles returns the number of data files holding fewer rows than maxRowsPerGroup \(candidates for CompactFiles\).

<a name="Dataset.OptimizeIndices"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [OptimizeIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L836>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) OptimizeIndices(ctx context.Context, opts ...OptimizeOption) error
```

OptimizeIndices updates the dataset's indices to cover data written since they were built. Upon success a new dataset version is committed and the handle tracks it.

<a name="Dataset.Paths"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Paths](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L364>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Paths(ctx context.Context) (DatasetPaths, error)
```

Paths returns the storage locations of the dataset.

<a name="Dataset.PlanCompaction"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [PlanCompaction](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L400>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) PlanCompaction(ctx context.Context, opts CompactionOptions) (CompactionPlan, error)
```

PlanCompaction plans a compaction without executing it and returns the plan: an independent CompactionTask per group of fragments to rewrite, plus the dataset version the plan was computed against. This is the planning half of a distributed compaction \(the "driver" step\); task execution and result commit are not yet exposed \(see the package notes\). opts.DeferIndexRemap is not a planning knob and is ignored \(see CompactFiles, which does honor it\).

<a name="Dataset.PrewarmIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [PrewarmIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L860>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) PrewarmIndex(ctx context.Context, name string) error
```

PrewarmIndex loads the index named name into memory ahead of queries. Fails with ErrNotFound if no index named name exists.

<a name="Dataset.PrewarmIndexWithOptions"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [PrewarmIndexWithOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L891>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) PrewarmIndexWithOptions(ctx context.Context, name string, opt PrewarmOption) error
```

PrewarmIndexWithOptions loads the index named name into memory ahead of queries, applying index\-type\-specific prewarm options \(e.g. PrewarmFTS\). Fails with ErrNotFound if no index named name exists.

<a name="Dataset.ReadIndexPartition"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ReadIndexPartition](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L291>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ReadIndexPartition(ctx context.Context, name string, partition uint64, withVector bool) (reader array.RecordReader, err error)
```

ReadIndexPartition streams the rows of one index partition. Set withVector to include the stored vectors. The caller must Release the reader.

<a name="Dataset.ReadTransaction"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ReadTransaction](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L490>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ReadTransaction(ctx context.Context) (res *Transaction, err error)
```

ReadTransaction returns the transaction that produced the currently checked\-out version, or \(nil, nil\) if none is recorded.

<a name="Dataset.RemapIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [RemapIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L1062>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) RemapIndex(ctx context.Context, column, name string) error
```

RemapIndex remaps the index on column to the row addresses moved by a prior compaction that ran with DeferIndexRemap \(whose moves are recorded in the fragment reuse index\). Upon success a new dataset version may be committed and the handle tracks it. When there is nothing to remap it is a no\-op. name is the index name, or empty for the Lance default \("\<column\>\_idx"\).

<a name="Dataset.ReplaceFieldMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ReplaceFieldMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L220>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ReplaceFieldMetadata(ctx context.Context, field string, metadata map[string]string) error
```

ReplaceFieldMetadata replaces the entire metadata map of the named field with metadata, committing a new version.

<a name="Dataset.ReplaceSchemaMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ReplaceSchemaMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L186>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ReplaceSchemaMetadata(ctx context.Context, metadata map[string]string) error
```

ReplaceSchemaMetadata replaces the entire schema metadata map with metadata, committing a new version.

<a name="Dataset.Restore"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Restore](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L113>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Restore(ctx context.Context) error
```

Restore commits the version currently checked out on this handle as the new latest version of the dataset. Typically used on a handle returned by Checkout to roll the dataset back. Other handles pick up the restored version via CheckoutLatest.

<a name="Dataset.SQL"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [SQL](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L36>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) SQL(query string) *SQLBuilder
```

SQL starts building a SQL query over the dataset, e.g. ds.SQL\("SELECT id FROM dataset WHERE id \> 10"\).Reader\(ctx\).

<a name="Dataset.Sample"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Sample](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L226>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Sample(ctx context.Context, n uint64, columns ...string) (arrow.RecordBatch, error)
```

Sample returns n randomly sampled rows \(in row\-id order, not random order\) projected onto columns \(all columns when empty\). When the dataset has fewer than n rows, all rows are returned. The caller must Release the result.

<a name="Dataset.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Scan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L110>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Scan() *Scanner
```

Scan starts building a scan over the dataset.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleDataset\_Scan writes a small dataset and then ranges over a filtered, ordered scan using Scanner.All, the range\-over\-func alternative to Reader.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-scan-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "score", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Float64},
	}, nil)
	b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
	defer b.Release()
	idB := b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder)
	scoreB := b.Field(1).(*array.Float64Builder)
	for i := int64(0); i < 10; i++ {
		idB.Append(i)
		scoreB.Append(float64(i) * 1.5)
	}
	rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
	defer rec.Release()
	rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	rdr.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer ds.Close()

	for batch, err := range ds.Scan().Filter("id >= 7").OrderBy("id").All(ctx) {
		if err != nil {
			log.Fatal(err)
		}
		ids := batch.Column(0).(*array.Int64)
		scores := batch.Column(1).(*array.Float64)
		for i := 0; i < int(batch.NumRows()); i++ {
			fmt.Printf("id=%d score=%.1f\n", ids.Value(i), scores.Value(i))
		}
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
id=7 score=10.5
id=8 score=12.0
id=9 score=13.5
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Dataset.Schema"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Schema](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L540>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Schema(ctx context.Context) (*arrow.Schema, error)
```

Schema returns the Arrow schema of the dataset.

<a name="Dataset.ShallowClone"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [ShallowClone](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L154>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) ShallowClone(ctx context.Context, targetURI string, ref Ref, storageOptions map[string]string) (*Dataset, error)
```

ShallowClone clones the referenced version \(or the currently checked\-out version for the zero Ref\) into a new dataset at targetURI without copying data files. The clone references the source dataset's files. Returns a handle to the clone, which the caller must Close. storageOptions configures the target's object store \(nil for none\).

<a name="Dataset.StorageOptions"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [StorageOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L94>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) StorageOptions(ctx context.Context) (map[string]string, error)
```

StorageOptions returns the object\-store options this dataset handle was opened with, as given \(an empty map when none were given\).

<a name="Dataset.Tags"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Tags](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L144>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Tags() *Tags
```

Tags returns the tag manager of the dataset. Tags are named references to versions. A tagged version is protected from CleanupOldVersions \(unless that behavior is disabled with WithErrorIfTaggedOldVersions\(false\)\).

<a name="Dataset.Take"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Take](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L118>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Take(ctx context.Context, spec TakeSpec) (arrow.RecordBatch, error)
```

Take returns the rows selected by spec as a single record batch, in request order. The caller must Release the result.

<a name="Dataset.TakeBlobs"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeBlobs](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L109>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeBlobs(ctx context.Context, column string, rowIDs []uint64) (*BlobList, error)
```

TakeBlobs returns blob\-file handles for the given stable row ids of a blob column. The caller must Close the returned BlobList.

<a name="Dataset.TakeBlobsByAddresses"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeBlobsByAddresses](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L123>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeBlobsByAddresses(ctx context.Context, column string, addresses []uint64) (*BlobList, error)
```

TakeBlobsByAddresses returns blob\-file handles for the given row addresses \(fragment\_id \<\< 32 | row\_offset\) of a blob column. The caller must Close the returned BlobList.

<a name="Dataset.TakeBlobsByIndices"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeBlobsByIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/blob.go#L116>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeBlobsByIndices(ctx context.Context, column string, indices []uint64) (*BlobList, error)
```

TakeBlobsByIndices returns blob\-file handles for the given row offsets \(0 = first live row\) of a blob column. The caller must Close the returned BlobList.

<a name="Dataset.TakeIndices"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L179>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeIndices(ctx context.Context, indices []uint64, columns ...string) (arrow.RecordBatch, error)
```

TakeIndices is a convenience for Take by row offsets: it returns the rows at the given positions \(0 = first live row\), projected onto columns \(all columns when empty\). The caller must Release the result.

<a name="Dataset.TakeRows"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L185>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeRows(ctx context.Context, rowIDs []uint64, columns ...string) (arrow.RecordBatch, error)
```

TakeRows is a convenience for Take by stable row ids. The caller must Release the result.

<a name="Dataset.TakeScan"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeScan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L194>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeScan(ctx context.Context, ranges [][2]uint64, columns ...string) (reader array.RecordReader, err error)
```

TakeScan streams the rows in the given half\-open \[start, end\) ranges of row offsets, projected onto columns \(all columns when empty\).

The returned reader and every record obtained from it must be Released by the caller. The reader stays valid after the Dataset is closed.

<a name="Dataset.TakeScanAll"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TakeScanAll](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L163>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TakeScanAll(ctx context.Context, ranges [][2]uint64, columns ...string) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

TakeScanAll streams the rows in the given half\-open \[start, end\) ranges of row offsets, projected onto columns \(all columns when empty\), as an iterator \- a range\-over\-func alternative to TakeScan.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the take scan, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="Dataset.TransactionByVersion"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TransactionByVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L500>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TransactionByVersion(ctx context.Context, v uint64) (res *Transaction, err error)
```

TransactionByVersion returns the transaction that produced version v, or \(nil, nil\) if none is recorded.

<a name="Dataset.Transactions"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Transactions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L510>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Transactions(ctx context.Context, n int) ([]*TransactionSummary, error)
```

Transactions returns up to n recent transaction summaries \(most recent first\). Entries that could not be read appear as nil.

<a name="Dataset.TruncateTable"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [TruncateTable](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L226>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) TruncateTable(ctx context.Context) error
```

TruncateTable deletes all rows from the dataset, committing a new version. Older versions remain accessible via time travel.

<a name="Dataset.Update"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Update](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L114>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Update(ctx context.Context, spec UpdateSpec) (res UpdateResult, err error)
```

Update modifies column values on rows matching spec.Where \(or all rows if empty\) and commits a new dataset version. The handle is updated in place to track the new version.

<a name="Dataset.UpdateConfig"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [UpdateConfig](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L109>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) UpdateConfig(ctx context.Context, updates map[string]string) (map[string]string, error)
```

UpdateConfig merges updates into the dataset configuration, committing a new version, and returns the resulting config map.

<a name="Dataset.UpdateFieldMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [UpdateFieldMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L214>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) UpdateFieldMetadata(ctx context.Context, field string, updates map[string]string) error
```

UpdateFieldMetadata merges updates into the metadata of the named field \(nested fields use dotted paths\), committing a new version.

<a name="Dataset.UpdateMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [UpdateMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L154>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) UpdateMetadata(ctx context.Context, updates map[string]string) (map[string]string, error)
```

UpdateMetadata merges updates into the table metadata, committing a new version, and returns the resulting metadata map.

<a name="Dataset.UpdateSchemaMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [UpdateSchemaMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L177>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) UpdateSchemaMetadata(ctx context.Context, updates map[string]string) (map[string]string, error)
```

UpdateSchemaMetadata merges updates into the schema metadata, committing a new version, and returns the resulting schema metadata map.

<a name="Dataset.Validate"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Validate](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L285>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Validate(ctx context.Context) error
```

Validate checks the dataset's internal consistency \(unique fragment ids, consistent field ids, physical row counts\) and returns an error when a check fails.

<a name="Dataset.Version"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Version](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L562>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Version(ctx context.Context) (VersionInfo, error)
```

Version returns information about the currently checked\-out version of the dataset.

<a name="Dataset.Versions"></a>
### func \(\*Dataset\) [Versions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L123>)

```go
func (d *Dataset) Versions(ctx context.Context) ([]VersionInfo, error)
```

Versions lists all committed versions of the dataset, oldest first.

<a name="DatasetPaths"></a>
## type [DatasetPaths](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L353-L361>)

DatasetPaths describes where a dataset stores its files. URI is fully qualified, the remaining values are object\-store paths without a scheme.

```go
type DatasetPaths struct {
    URI         string `json:"uri"`
    Base        string `json:"base"`
    DataDir     string `json:"data_dir"`
    IndicesDir  string `json:"indices_dir"`
    VersionsDir string `json:"versions_dir"`
    // Branch is the checked-out branch, or "" for the main branch.
    Branch string `json:"branch"`
}
```

<a name="DeleteResult"></a>
## type [DeleteResult](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L26-L29>)

DeleteResult reports the outcome of a Delete.

```go
type DeleteResult struct {
    // NumDeletedRows is the number of rows removed by the predicate.
    NumDeletedRows uint64 `json:"num_deleted_rows"`
}
```

<a name="DeletionFileInfo"></a>
## type [DeletionFileInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L50-L57>)

DeletionFileInfo describes a fragment's deletion file, if any.

```go
type DeletionFileInfo struct {
    // ReadVersion is the dataset version the deletion file was written at.
    ReadVersion uint64 `json:"read_version"`
    // ID is the deletion file id.
    ID  uint64 `json:"id"`
    // NumDeletedRows is the number of deleted rows, if recorded.
    NumDeletedRows *uint64 `json:"num_deleted_rows"`
}
```

<a name="DeltaBuilder"></a>
## type [DeltaBuilder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L25-L28>)

DeltaBuilder configures a change\-data\-capture query between two dataset versions. Build one with Dataset.Delta, select a range with exactly one of ComparedAgainstVersion, FromVersion\+ToVersion, or FromDate\+ToDate, then call one of the terminal methods.

The begin bound is exclusive and the end bound inclusive: a delta from version 1 to version 3 covers the changes committed by versions 2 and 3.

```go
type DeltaBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="DeltaBuilder.AllInserted"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [AllInserted](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L114>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) AllInserted(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

AllInserted streams the rows created within the delta's version range as an iterator, a range\-over\-func alternative to InsertedRows. See InsertedRows for the result shape.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the query, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.AllUpdated"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [AllUpdated](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L130>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) AllUpdated(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

AllUpdated streams the rows updated within the delta's version range as an iterator, a range\-over\-func alternative to UpdatedRows. See InsertedRows for the result shape.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the query, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.AllUpserted"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [AllUpserted](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L146>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) AllUpserted(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

AllUpserted streams both the inserted and the updated rows of the delta's version range as an iterator, a range\-over\-func alternative to UpsertedRows. See InsertedRows for the result shape.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the query, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.ComparedAgainstVersion"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [ComparedAgainstVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L67>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) ComparedAgainstVersion(v uint64) *DeltaBuilder
```

ComparedAgainstVersion compares the checked\-out version against version v \(the bounds are ordered automatically\). Mutually exclusive with the explicit ranges.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.FromDate"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [FromDate](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L86>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) FromDate(t time.Time) *DeltaBuilder
```

FromDate sets the exclusive begin timestamp. Use together with ToDate.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.FromVersion"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [FromVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L74>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) FromVersion(v uint64) *DeltaBuilder
```

FromVersion sets the exclusive begin version. Use together with ToVersion.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.InsertedRows"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [InsertedRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L128>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) InsertedRows(ctx context.Context) (array.RecordReader, error)
```

InsertedRows streams the rows created within the delta's version range. Results include the \_rowid, \_row\_created\_at\_version and \_row\_last\_updated\_at\_version columns. The returned reader and its records must be Released by the caller.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.ToDate"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [ToDate](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L92>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) ToDate(t time.Time) *DeltaBuilder
```

ToDate sets the inclusive end timestamp. Use together with FromDate.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.ToVersion"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [ToVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L80>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) ToVersion(v uint64) *DeltaBuilder
```

ToVersion sets the inclusive end version. Use together with FromVersion.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.Transactions"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [Transactions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L146>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) Transactions(ctx context.Context) ([]TransactionInfo, error)
```

Transactions lists the transactions committed within the delta's version range, oldest first.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.UpdatedRows"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [UpdatedRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L134>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) UpdatedRows(ctx context.Context) (array.RecordReader, error)
```

UpdatedRows streams the rows that existed before the delta's version range and were updated within it. See InsertedRows for the result shape.

<a name="DeltaBuilder.UpsertedRows"></a>
### func \(\*DeltaBuilder\) [UpsertedRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L140>)

```go
func (b *DeltaBuilder) UpsertedRows(ctx context.Context) (array.RecordReader, error)
```

UpsertedRows streams both the inserted and the updated rows of the delta's version range. See InsertedRows for the result shape.

<a name="DetachedManifest"></a>
## type [DetachedManifest](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L604-L611>)

DetachedManifest describes a detached manifest: a manifest committed outside the dataset's normal lineage \(see CommitBuilder.Detached\). Version carries the detached high bit. For the naming scheme and ETag of a freshly committed detached version, use Dataset.ManifestLocation on the handle returned by Execute.

```go
type DetachedManifest struct {
    // Version is the detached version number (carries the detached high bit).
    Version uint64 `json:"version"`
    // Path is the object-store path of the detached manifest file.
    Path string `json:"path"`
    // Size is the manifest file's size in bytes, if known.
    Size *uint64 `json:"size"`
}
```

<a name="DistanceType"></a>
## type [DistanceType](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L22>)

DistanceType selects the metric used to compare vectors.

```go
type DistanceType int
```

<a name="L2"></a>

```go
const (
    // L2 is Euclidean distance (the default).
    L2  DistanceType = iota
    // Cosine is cosine distance.
    Cosine
    // Dot is negative dot-product distance.
    Dot
    // Hamming is Hamming distance (for binary vectors).
    Hamming
)
```

<a name="DistanceType.String"></a>
### func \(DistanceType\) [String](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L37>)

```go
func (d DistanceType) String() string
```

String returns the metric's wire representation \("l2", "cosine", "dot", or "hamming"\).

<a name="ExternalBlobMode"></a>
## type [ExternalBlobMode](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L57>)

ExternalBlobMode selects how external blob URIs are handled on write.

```go
type ExternalBlobMode string
```

<a name="ExternalBlobReference"></a>

```go
const (
    // ExternalBlobReference stores the URI as an external blob reference
    // (the default).
    ExternalBlobReference ExternalBlobMode = "reference"
    // ExternalBlobIngest reads the external bytes during the write and
    // stores them in Lance-managed storage.
    ExternalBlobIngest ExternalBlobMode = "ingest"
)
```

<a name="FM"></a>
## type [FM](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L151>)

FM configures an FM\-index, which accelerates substring \`contains\` filters with exact matches.

```go
type FM struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FieldStatistics"></a>
## type [FieldStatistics](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/config.go#L450-L456>)

FieldStatistics describes the storage of a single field.

```go
type FieldStatistics struct {
    // ID is the field id (matching the dataset schema).
    ID  uint32 `json:"id"`
    // BytesOnDisk is the compressed size of the field's data. Zero for
    // datasets written with a data storage version below 2.
    BytesOnDisk uint64 `json:"bytes_on_disk"`
}
```

<a name="Fragment"></a>
## type [Fragment](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L76-L86>)

Fragment is a handle to a single fragment of a dataset. It is self\-contained \(it snapshots the dataset\), so it stays valid after the originating Dataset is closed. Close must be called to release native resources. It is idempotent and safe for concurrent use.

```go
type Fragment struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Fragment.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [Close](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L140>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) Close() error
```

Close releases the native fragment handle. It is idempotent. Streams from a fragment Scan remain valid after Close.

<a name="Fragment.CountDeletions"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [CountDeletions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L181>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) CountDeletions(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
```

CountDeletions returns the number of rows deleted from the fragment.

<a name="Fragment.CountRows"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [CountRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L165>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) CountRows(ctx context.Context, filter string) (uint64, error)
```

CountRows returns the number of live rows in the fragment. A non\-empty filter restricts the count to rows matching the SQL predicate.

<a name="Fragment.Metadata"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [Metadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L210>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) Metadata(ctx context.Context) (FragmentMetadata, error)
```

Metadata returns the fragment's decoded metadata.

<a name="Fragment.PhysicalRows"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [PhysicalRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L196>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) PhysicalRows(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
```

PhysicalRows returns the number of physical rows stored in the fragment \(before deletions\).

<a name="Fragment.Scan"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [Scan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L290>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) Scan() *FragmentScanner
```

Scan starts building a scan over the fragment.

<a name="Fragment.Take"></a>
### func \(\*Fragment\) [Take](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L237>)

```go
func (f *Fragment) Take(ctx context.Context, indices []uint32, columns ...string) (res arrow.RecordBatch, err error)
```

Take returns the rows at the given in\-fragment offsets as a single record batch, projected onto columns \(all columns when none given\). The caller must Release the result.

<a name="FragmentInfo"></a>
## type [FragmentInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L21-L35>)

FragmentInfo summarizes one fragment of a dataset \(see Dataset.Fragments\).

```go
type FragmentInfo struct {
    // ID is the fragment id.
    ID  uint32 `json:"id"`
    // NumRows is the number of live (non-deleted) rows in the fragment.
    NumRows uint64 `json:"num_rows"`
    // PhysicalRows is the number of rows stored before deletions.
    PhysicalRows uint64 `json:"physical_rows"`
    // NumDeletions is the number of rows deleted from the fragment.
    NumDeletions uint64 `json:"num_deletions"`
    // NumDataFiles is the number of data files backing the fragment.
    NumDataFiles uint32 `json:"num_data_files"`
    // DataFiles lists the fragment's data file paths, relative to the
    // dataset root.
    DataFiles []string `json:"data_files"`
}
```

<a name="FragmentMetadata"></a>
## type [FragmentMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L61-L70>)

FragmentMetadata is the decoded metadata of a fragment \(see Fragment.Metadata\). Fields not modeled here are ignored on decode.

```go
type FragmentMetadata struct {
    // ID is the fragment id.
    ID  uint64 `json:"id"`
    // PhysicalRows is the stored row count, if recorded in the manifest.
    PhysicalRows *uint64 `json:"physical_rows"`
    // Files lists the fragment's data files.
    Files []DataFileInfo `json:"files"`
    // DeletionFile describes the fragment's deletion file, if any.
    DeletionFile *DeletionFileInfo `json:"deletion_file"`
}
```

<a name="FragmentScanner"></a>
## type [FragmentScanner](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L284-L287>)

FragmentScanner builds a scan restricted to one fragment. It mirrors the subset of Scanner knobs that fragment scans support. Configure it with the chained methods, then call the terminal Reader.

```go
type FragmentScanner struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FragmentScanner.All"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [All](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L98>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) All(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

All executes the fragment scan and returns an iterator over the result batches, as a range\-over\-func alternative to Reader.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the scan, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="FragmentScanner.BatchSize"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [BatchSize](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L335>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) BatchSize(n uint64) *FragmentScanner
```

BatchSize caps the number of rows per emitted record batch.

<a name="FragmentScanner.Columns"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [Columns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L299>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) Columns(columns ...string) *FragmentScanner
```

Columns restricts the scan to the given columns \(all columns by default\).

<a name="FragmentScanner.Filter"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [Filter](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L305>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) Filter(filter string) *FragmentScanner
```

Filter applies an SQL predicate, e.g. "id \< 50".

<a name="FragmentScanner.Limit"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [Limit](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L311>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) Limit(limit int64) *FragmentScanner
```

Limit caps the number of rows returned.

<a name="FragmentScanner.Offset"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [Offset](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L317>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) Offset(offset int64) *FragmentScanner
```

Offset skips the first offset rows.

<a name="FragmentScanner.Reader"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [Reader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L350>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) Reader(ctx context.Context) (reader array.RecordReader, err error)
```

Reader executes the fragment scan and returns a reader over the result batches. The reader and every record obtained from it must be Released by the caller. The reader stays valid after the Fragment and Dataset are closed.

<a name="FragmentScanner.ScanInOrder"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [ScanInOrder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L341>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) ScanInOrder(ordered bool) *FragmentScanner
```

ScanInOrder controls whether batches are returned in stable row order.

<a name="FragmentScanner.WithRowAddress"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [WithRowAddress](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L329>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) WithRowAddress() *FragmentScanner
```

WithRowAddress includes the internal \_rowaddr column in the results.

<a name="FragmentScanner.WithRowID"></a>
### func \(\*FragmentScanner\) [WithRowID](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/fragment.go#L323>)

```go
func (s *FragmentScanner) WithRowID() *FragmentScanner
```

WithRowID includes the internal \_rowid column in the results.

<a name="FtsOperator"></a>
## type [FtsOperator](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L814>)

FtsOperator combines the terms of a MatchQuery.

```go
type FtsOperator string
```

<a name="FtsOperatorOr"></a>

```go
const (
    // FtsOperatorOr matches rows containing at least one term (default).
    FtsOperatorOr FtsOperator = "Or"
    // FtsOperatorAnd matches only rows containing all terms.
    FtsOperatorAnd FtsOperator = "And"
)
```

<a name="FtsOption"></a>
## type [FtsOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L550>)

FtsOption configures FullTextSearch.

```go
type FtsOption func(*scanConfig)
```

<a name="WithFtsColumns"></a>
### func [WithFtsColumns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L554>)

```go
func WithFtsColumns(columns ...string) FtsOption
```

WithFtsColumns sets the columns to search when the query itself does not name them.

<a name="WithFtsLimit"></a>
### func [WithFtsLimit](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L559>)

```go
func WithFtsLimit(limit int64) FtsOption
```

WithFtsLimit caps the number of full\-text search results.

<a name="WithWandFactor"></a>
### func [WithWandFactor](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L565>)

```go
func WithWandFactor(factor float32) FtsOption
```

WithWandFactor tunes the WAND ranking factor \(default 1.0\). Larger values trade recall for speed.

<a name="FtsQuery"></a>
## type [FtsQuery](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L795-L799>)

FtsQuery is a full\-text search query node. Implementations: MatchQuery, PhraseQuery, BoostQuery, MultiMatchQuery, BooleanQuery.

```go
type FtsQuery interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="IndexConfig"></a>
## type [IndexConfig](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L59-L71>)

IndexConfig selects the type of index to create and its build parameters. Implementations: BTree, Bitmap, LabelList, NGram, ZoneMap, BloomFilter, FM, RTree, JSONIndex, Inverted, IvfFlat, IvfPq, IvfSq, IvfRq, IvfHnswFlat, IvfHnswPq, IvfHnswSq. Zero values map to the Lance defaults.

The interface methods are unexported, so IndexConfig cannot be implemented outside this package, so the set of index types is closed.

```go
type IndexConfig interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="IndexCriteria"></a>
## type [IndexCriteria](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L936-L946>)

IndexCriteria filters the indices considered by DescribeIndices.

```go
type IndexCriteria struct {
    // ForColumn restricts to indices over this single column.
    ForColumn string `json:"for_column,omitempty"`
    // HasName restricts to the index with this name.
    HasName string `json:"has_name,omitempty"`
    // MustSupportFTS restricts to indices that support full-text search.
    MustSupportFTS bool `json:"must_support_fts,omitempty"`
    // MustSupportExactEquality restricts to indices that answer exact
    // equality (excludes ngram/inverted/bloom-filter).
    MustSupportExactEquality bool `json:"must_support_exact_equality,omitempty"`
}
```

<a name="IndexDescription"></a>
## type [IndexDescription](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L911-L933>)

IndexDescription describes an index of a dataset \(its type, coverage and details\), as returned by DescribeIndices. It mirrors the lance\_index IndexDescription trait.

```go
type IndexDescription struct {
    // Name is the index name.
    Name string `json:"name"`
    // IndexType is a short type identifier (e.g. "BTree", "IVF_PQ", "Json"),
    // or "Unknown" if no plugin recognizes the index details.
    IndexType string `json:"index_type"`
    // TypeURL is the protobuf type URL of the index details.
    TypeURL string `json:"type_url"`
    // RowsIndexed is the approximate number of rows the index covers across
    // all segments (may include deleted rows).
    RowsIndexed uint64 `json:"rows_indexed"`
    // FieldIDs are the dataset field ids the index is built on.
    FieldIDs []int32 `json:"field_ids"`
    // Details is an index-type-specific JSON document, or empty when it could
    // not be decoded (e.g. no plugin for the type).
    Details string `json:"details,omitempty"`
    // TotalSizeBytes is the total on-disk size of the index across all
    // segments, or nil when size information is unavailable.
    TotalSizeBytes *uint64 `json:"total_size_bytes,omitempty"`
    // Segments are the physical index segments that make up this logical
    // index.
    Segments []IndexInfo `json:"segments"`
}
```

<a name="IndexInfo"></a>
## type [IndexInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L628-L641>)

IndexInfo describes an index of a dataset.

```go
type IndexInfo struct {
    // Name is the index name.
    Name string `json:"name"`
    // UUID uniquely identifies this index build.
    UUID string `json:"uuid"`
    // Fields are the dataset field ids covered by the index.
    Fields []int32 `json:"fields"`
    // DatasetVersion is the dataset version the index was last updated on.
    DatasetVersion uint64 `json:"dataset_version"`
    // IndexVersion is the index format version.
    IndexVersion int32 `json:"index_version"`
    // CreatedAt is the index creation time, if recorded.
    CreatedAt *time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
}
```

<a name="IndexMetadata"></a>
## type [IndexMetadata](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L22-L28>)

IndexMetadata is an uncommitted index segment produced by CreateIndexUncommitted or MergeIndexSegments. Like Transaction, it holds a lossless protobuf encoding \(used to merge/commit the segment, preserving index details a JSON view would drop\) plus a JSON summary for inspection.

```go
type IndexMetadata struct {

    // Raw is the full JSON summary of the segment.
    Raw json.RawMessage
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="IndexMetadata.Bytes"></a>
### func \(\*IndexMetadata\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L65>)

```go
func (m *IndexMetadata) Bytes() []byte
```

Bytes returns a copy of the opaque, lossless protobuf encoding of the segment.

<a name="IndexMetadata.Name"></a>
### func \(\*IndexMetadata\) [Name](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L47>)

```go
func (m *IndexMetadata) Name() string
```

Name is the index name.

<a name="IndexMetadata.UUID"></a>
### func \(\*IndexMetadata\) [UUID](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L50>)

```go
func (m *IndexMetadata) UUID() string
```

UUID is the segment's build UUID.

<a name="IndexMetadata.View"></a>
### func \(\*IndexMetadata\) [View](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L53>)

```go
func (m *IndexMetadata) View() IndexMetadataView
```

View returns a copy of the typed summary of the segment.

<a name="IndexMetadataView"></a>
## type [IndexMetadataView](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L31-L44>)

IndexMetadataView is a typed summary of an index segment.

```go
type IndexMetadataView struct {
    // Name is the index name.
    Name string `json:"name"`
    // UUID is the segment's build UUID.
    UUID string `json:"uuid"`
    // Fields lists the field ids the index covers.
    Fields []int32 `json:"fields"`
    // DatasetVersion is the dataset version the segment was built against.
    DatasetVersion uint64 `json:"dataset_version"`
    // IndexVersion is the on-disk index format version.
    IndexVersion int32 `json:"index_version"`
    // CreatedAt is when the segment was created, RFC3339, if reported.
    CreatedAt *string `json:"created_at"`
}
```

<a name="IndexOption"></a>
## type [IndexOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L650>)

IndexOption configures CreateIndex.

```go
type IndexOption func(*indexOptions)
```

<a name="WithIndexName"></a>
### func [WithIndexName](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L653>)

```go
func WithIndexName(name string) IndexOption
```

WithIndexName names the index \(default "\<column\>\_idx"\).

<a name="WithReplace"></a>
### func [WithReplace](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L659>)

```go
func WithReplace(replace bool) IndexOption
```

WithReplace controls whether an existing index with the same name is replaced \(default false: CreateIndex fails if the name is taken\).

<a name="Inverted"></a>
## type [Inverted](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L235-L267>)

Inverted configures an inverted \(full\-text search\) index. The zero value uses the Lance defaults: "simple" tokenizer, English, lower\-casing, stemming, stop\-word removal and ascii folding enabled, no positions.

```go
type Inverted struct {
    // BaseTokenizer is "simple" (default), "whitespace", "raw", "ngram",
    // "icu", "lindera/*" or "jieba/*".
    BaseTokenizer string
    // Language for stemming and stop words, e.g. "English" (default).
    Language string
    // WithPosition stores token positions, enabling phrase queries.
    WithPosition bool
    // LowerCase lower-cases tokens (default true).
    LowerCase *bool
    // Stem applies stemming (default true).
    Stem *bool
    // RemoveStopWords removes stop words (default true).
    RemoveStopWords *bool
    // AsciiFolding converts accented characters to their ascii equivalent
    // (default true).
    AsciiFolding *bool
    // MaxTokenLength removes tokens longer than the limit (default 40).
    // Point at 0 for "no limit".
    MaxTokenLength *int
    // CustomStopWords replaces the language's built-in stop-word list.
    CustomStopWords []string
    // NgramMinLength is the minimum n-gram length ("ngram" tokenizer only,
    // default 3).
    NgramMinLength uint32
    // NgramMaxLength is the maximum n-gram length ("ngram" tokenizer only,
    // default 3).
    NgramMaxLength uint32
    // NgramPrefixOnly generates only prefix n-grams ("ngram" tokenizer
    // only).
    NgramPrefixOnly bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="IvfFlat"></a>
## type [IvfFlat](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L455-L461>)

IvfFlat configures an IVF\_FLAT vector index \(exact distances within probed partitions\).

```go
type IvfFlat struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfHnswFlat"></a>
## type [IvfHnswFlat](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L543-L554>)

IvfHnswFlat configures an IVF\_HNSW\_FLAT vector index.

```go
type IvfHnswFlat struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    // M is the number of HNSW connections per node (default 20).
    M   int
    // EfConstruction is the HNSW build-time candidate list size
    // (default 150).
    EfConstruction int
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfHnswPq"></a>
## type [IvfHnswPq](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L567-L582>)

IvfHnswPq configures an IVF\_HNSW\_PQ vector index.

```go
type IvfHnswPq struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Bits per PQ centroid (default 8).
    Bits int
    // SubVectors is the number of PQ sub-vectors (default 16).
    SubVectors int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    // M is the number of HNSW connections per node (default 20).
    M   int
    // EfConstruction is the HNSW build-time candidate list size
    // (default 150).
    EfConstruction int
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfHnswSq"></a>
## type [IvfHnswSq](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L599-L612>)

IvfHnswSq configures an IVF\_HNSW\_SQ vector index.

```go
type IvfHnswSq struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Bits of scaling range (default 8).
    Bits int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    // M is the number of HNSW connections per node (default 20).
    M   int
    // EfConstruction is the HNSW build-time candidate list size
    // (default 150).
    EfConstruction int
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfPq"></a>
## type [IvfPq](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L470-L483>)

IvfPq configures an IVF\_PQ vector index \(product quantization\).

```go
type IvfPq struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Bits per PQ centroid (default 8).
    Bits int
    // SubVectors is the number of PQ sub-vectors (default 16). Must divide
    // the vector dimension.
    SubVectors int
    // MaxIterations caps k-means training iterations (default 50).
    MaxIterations int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfRq"></a>
## type [IvfRq](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L520-L530>)

IvfRq configures an IVF\_RQ vector index \(RaBitQ quantization\).

```go
type IvfRq struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Bits per dimension (default 1).
    Bits int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    // RotationType is "fast" (default) or "matrix".
    RotationType string
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="IvfSq"></a>
## type [IvfSq](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L497-L507>)

IvfSq configures an IVF\_SQ vector index \(scalar quantization\).

```go
type IvfSq struct {
    // Partitions is the number of IVF partitions (0 = auto).
    Partitions int
    // Bits of scaling range (default 8).
    Bits int
    // SampleRate for training (default 256).
    SampleRate int
    // Distance is the metric to index with (default L2).
    Distance DistanceType
    VectorOptions
}
```

<a name="JSONIndex"></a>
## type [JSONIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L182-L192>)

JSONIndex configures a JSON scalar index: it indexes the value at a JSON path within a JSON column, delegating to a target scalar index type. The column must be a JSON column \(an arrow.json / lance.json extension field, i.e. LargeBinary JSONB storage\).

```go
type JSONIndex struct {
    // Path is the JSON path to index, e.g. "x" or "$.user.name".
    Path string
    // TargetIndexType is the underlying scalar index type built over the
    // extracted values, e.g. "btree" or "bitmap".
    TargetIndexType string
    // TargetParams is an optional JSON parameter document (as a string)
    // passed to the target index type, empty for its defaults.
    TargetParams string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="LabelList"></a>
## type [LabelList](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L98>)

LabelList configures a LabelList index over list\-of\-labels columns.

```go
type LabelList struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ManifestField"></a>
## type [ManifestField](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L578-L583>)

ManifestField describes one schema field in a manifest.

```go
type ManifestField struct {
    // ID is the field id.
    ID  int32 `json:"id"`
    // Name is the field name.
    Name string `json:"name"`
}
```

<a name="ManifestInfo"></a>
## type [ManifestInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L559-L575>)

ManifestInfo is a summary of a dataset manifest.

```go
type ManifestInfo struct {
    // Version is the dataset version the manifest backs.
    Version uint64 `json:"version"`
    // Fields lists the manifest's schema fields.
    Fields []ManifestField `json:"fields"`
    // NumFragments is the number of fragments in this version.
    NumFragments int `json:"num_fragments"`
    // Config holds the dataset configuration key-value map.
    Config map[string]string `json:"config"`
    // TableMetadata holds the table metadata key-value map.
    TableMetadata map[string]string `json:"table_metadata"`
    // Tag is the tag name pointing at this version, if any.
    Tag *string `json:"tag"`
    // Branch is the branch this version belongs to, or nil for the main
    // branch.
    Branch *string `json:"branch"`
}
```

<a name="ManifestLocation"></a>
## type [ManifestLocation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L285-L296>)

ManifestLocation describes where a manifest is stored.

```go
type ManifestLocation struct {
    // Version is the dataset version the manifest backs.
    Version uint64 `json:"version"`
    // Path is the object-store path of the manifest file.
    Path string `json:"path"`
    // Size is the manifest file's size in bytes, if known.
    Size *uint64 `json:"size"`
    // NamingScheme is the manifest path naming scheme: "V1" or "V2".
    NamingScheme string `json:"naming_scheme"`
    // ETag is the object-store entity tag of the manifest file, if reported.
    ETag *string `json:"e_tag"`
}
```

<a name="MatchQuery"></a>
## type [MatchQuery](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L828-L848>)

MatchQuery matches rows whose column contains the query terms.

```go
type MatchQuery struct {
    // Column to search. Empty means "determined by the search" (set via
    // WithFtsColumns or the only indexed column).
    Column string
    // Terms is the query text. It is tokenized like the indexed documents.
    Terms string
    // Boost scales the score contribution (default 1.0).
    Boost float32
    // Fuzziness is the max edit distance per term: 0 exact (default), n > 0
    // for that distance, or FuzzinessAuto.
    Fuzziness int
    // MaxExpansions caps the number of terms considered for fuzzy matching
    // (default 50).
    MaxExpansions int
    // Operator combines the terms: FtsOperatorOr (default) or
    // FtsOperatorAnd.
    Operator FtsOperator
    // PrefixLength is the number of leading characters kept unchanged by
    // fuzzy matching.
    PrefixLength uint32
}
```

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder"></a>
## type [MergeInsertBuilder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L195-L198>)

MergeInsertBuilder configures a merge\-insert \(upsert / find\-or\-create\) operation joining source rows against the dataset on the given key columns. Configure it with the chained When\* methods, then call Execute.

The defaults mirror Lance's find\-or\-create semantics: matched target rows are kept as\-is \(WhenMatchedDoNothing\), unmatched source rows are inserted \(WhenNotMatchedInsertAll\), and target rows missing from the source are kept \(WhenNotMatchedBySourceKeep\), with 10 conflict retries over at most 30 seconds.

A MergeInsertBuilder must not be used concurrently while it is being configured.

```go
type MergeInsertBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.CommitRetries"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [CommitRetries](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L317>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) CommitRetries(n uint32) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

CommitRetries sets the number of inner commit retries for manifest version conflicts \(the Lance default is 20\). This is distinct from ConflictRetries, which retries the whole operation on semantic write contention.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.ConflictRetries"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [ConflictRetries](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L283>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) ConflictRetries(n uint32) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

ConflictRetries overrides the number of times the operation is retried on commit contention \(the Lance default is 10\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.Execute"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [Execute](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L339>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) Execute(ctx context.Context, rdr array.RecordReader) (res MergeStats, err error)
```

Execute runs the merge\-insert with rdr as the source rows and commits a new dataset version. The handle is updated in place to track the new version. The reader is fully consumed. The caller keeps its own reference and should still Release it as usual.

As with Write, the reader's batches cross the Arrow C Data Interface, so their buffers must live outside the Go heap \(use a C\-backed allocator such as Allocator\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.RetryTimeout"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [RetryTimeout](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L290>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) RetryTimeout(d time.Duration) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

RetryTimeout caps the total time spent on retries \(the Lance default is 30 seconds\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.SkipAutoCleanup"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [SkipAutoCleanup](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L326>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) SkipAutoCleanup(skip bool) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

SkipAutoCleanup controls whether automatic cleanup of old versions during the commit is skipped. The Lance default is false. Setting it to true can improve throughput for high\-frequency writers or writers without delete permissions.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.SourceDedupeBehavior"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [SourceDedupeBehavior](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L300>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) SourceDedupeBehavior(behavior SourceDedupeBehavior) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

SourceDedupeBehavior controls how duplicate source rows that match the same target row on the join key are handled. The Lance default is SourceDedupeFail, which errors on such duplicates. SourceDedupeFirstSeen keeps the first\-seen source row and skips the rest, reporting the skipped count in MergeStats.NumSkippedDuplicates.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.UseIndex"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [UseIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L309>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) UseIndex(use bool) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

UseIndex controls whether a scalar index on the join key is used to find matches. The Lance default is true. Passing false forces a full table scan even when an index exists \(useful for benchmarking or to sidestep a suboptimal plan\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenMatchedDelete"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenMatchedDelete](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L226>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenMatchedDelete() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenMatchedDelete removes matched target rows \(bulk delete by key\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenMatchedDoNothing"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenMatchedDoNothing](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L240>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenMatchedDoNothing() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenMatchedDoNothing keeps matched target rows unchanged \(the default, find\-or\-create\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenMatchedFail"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenMatchedFail](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L233>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenMatchedFail() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenMatchedFail fails the operation if any source row matches an existing target row.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenMatchedUpdateAll"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenMatchedUpdateAll](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L212>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenMatchedUpdateAll() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenMatchedUpdateAll replaces matched target rows with the source row \(upsert\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenMatchedUpdateIf"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenMatchedUpdateIf](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L220>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenMatchedUpdateIf(expr string) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenMatchedUpdateIf replaces matched target rows with the source row only where expr evaluates to true. The expression references the joined row via "target." and "source." qualifiers, e.g. "source.score \> target.score".

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenNotMatchedBySourceDelete"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenNotMatchedBySourceDelete](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L268>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenNotMatchedBySourceDelete() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenNotMatchedBySourceDelete removes target rows that have no matching source row.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenNotMatchedBySourceDeleteIf"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenNotMatchedBySourceDeleteIf](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L276>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenNotMatchedBySourceDeleteIf(expr string) *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenNotMatchedBySourceDeleteIf removes target rows that have no matching source row and for which expr \(an SQL predicate over the target columns\) evaluates to true.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenNotMatchedBySourceKeep"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenNotMatchedBySourceKeep](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L261>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenNotMatchedBySourceKeep() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenNotMatchedBySourceKeep keeps target rows that have no matching source row \(the default\).

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenNotMatchedDoNothing"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenNotMatchedDoNothing](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L254>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenNotMatchedDoNothing() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenNotMatchedDoNothing ignores source rows that have no matching target row.

<a name="MergeInsertBuilder.WhenNotMatchedInsertAll"></a>
### func \(\*MergeInsertBuilder\) [WhenNotMatchedInsertAll](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L247>)

```go
func (b *MergeInsertBuilder) WhenNotMatchedInsertAll() *MergeInsertBuilder
```

WhenNotMatchedInsertAll inserts source rows that have no matching target row \(the default\).

<a name="MergeStats"></a>
## type [MergeStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L54-L69>)

MergeStats reports the outcome of a MergeInsert.

```go
type MergeStats struct {
    // NumInsertedRows is the number of source rows inserted.
    NumInsertedRows uint64 `json:"num_inserted_rows"`
    // NumUpdatedRows is the number of target rows updated from the source.
    NumUpdatedRows uint64 `json:"num_updated_rows"`
    // NumDeletedRows is the number of target rows deleted.
    NumDeletedRows uint64 `json:"num_deleted_rows"`
    // NumAttempts is the number of attempts performed (>1 on contention).
    NumAttempts uint32 `json:"num_attempts"`
    // BytesWritten is the total data-file bytes written to storage.
    BytesWritten uint64 `json:"bytes_written"`
    // NumFilesWritten is the number of data files written.
    NumFilesWritten uint64 `json:"num_files_written"`
    // NumSkippedDuplicates is the number of duplicate source rows skipped.
    NumSkippedDuplicates uint64 `json:"num_skipped_duplicates"`
}
```

## type [MultiMatchQuery](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L947-L949>)

MultiMatchQuery runs the same terms against several columns. Every sub\-query must name a Column and share the same Terms. Per\-column Boost values are honored.

```go
type MultiMatchQuery struct {
    Queries []MatchQuery
}
```
