NGram configures an NGram index, which accelerates \`contains\` filters.

```go
type NGram struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NamedExpr"></a>
## type [NamedExpr](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/evolution.go#L21-L26>)

NamedExpr defines a new column as an SQL expression evaluated over the existing columns of the dataset.

```go
type NamedExpr struct {
    // Name is the name of the new column.
    Name string
    // SQL is the expression computing the column's values, e.g. "score * 2".
    SQL string
}
```

<a name="ObjectStoreCache"></a>
## type [ObjectStoreCache](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/cache.go#L53-L59>)

ObjectStoreCache is a byte\-range cache for immutable Lance file reads, attached to an opened dataset via OpenWithObjectStoreCache. Keys are \(path, start, end\). Lance data files are write\-once so no invalidation is needed. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.

```go
type ObjectStoreCache interface {
    // Get returns the cached bytes for the exact (path, start, end) range and
    // whether they were found.
    Get(path string, start, end uint64) (data []byte, found bool)
    // Put stores data for the (path, start, end) range.
    Put(path string, start, end uint64, data []byte)
}
```

<a name="ObsOption"></a>
## type [ObsOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L229>)

ObsOption configures the OpenTelemetry providers a handle uses for tracing, metrics and logging. Pass ObsOptions to constructors via WithObservability \(Open\), WithWriteObservability \(Write\), or directly to NewSession. When no provider is supplied the corresponding OTel global is used, which is a no\-op until the application installs an SDK.

```go
type ObsOption func(*obsConfig)
```

<a name="WithLoggerProvider"></a>
### func [WithLoggerProvider](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L245>)

```go
func WithLoggerProvider(lp otellog.LoggerProvider) ObsOption
```

WithLoggerProvider sets the OpenTelemetry LoggerProvider \(default: the OTel global logger provider\).

<a name="WithMeterProvider"></a>
### func [WithMeterProvider](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L239>)

```go
func WithMeterProvider(mp metric.MeterProvider) ObsOption
```

WithMeterProvider sets the OpenTelemetry MeterProvider \(default: the OTel global meter provider\).

<a name="WithTracerProvider"></a>
### func [WithTracerProvider](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L233>)

```go
func WithTracerProvider(tp trace.TracerProvider) ObsOption
```

WithTracerProvider sets the OpenTelemetry TracerProvider \(default: the OTel global tracer provider\).

<a name="OpenOption"></a>
## type [OpenOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L137>)

OpenOption configures Open. The concrete options are produced by the With\* constructors \(WithVersion, WithTag, WithSession, WithObjectStoreCache, ...\).

```go
type OpenOption func(*openConfig)
```

<a name="WithBranch"></a>
### func [WithBranch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/branch.go#L22>)

```go
func WithBranch(name string) OpenOption
```

WithBranch opens the dataset checked out on the named branch \(at its latest version, or at a specific version when combined with WithVersion\).

<a name="WithObjectStoreCache"></a>
### func [WithObjectStoreCache](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/cache.go#L156>)

```go
func WithObjectStoreCache(cache ObjectStoreCache) OpenOption
```

WithObjectStoreCache wraps the opened dataset's object store so that immutable file byte\-range reads are served through cache. It composes with the other Open options, including WithSession, so a dataset can be opened with a shared session AND a byte cache at once. A nil cache is a no\-op.

Native dataset/scanner/reader clones retain the cache registration until the last clone is released. This is an Open\-only option: writes have no object\-store wrapper hook.

<a name="WithObservability"></a>
### func [WithObservability](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L251>)

```go
func WithObservability(opts ...ObsOption) OpenOption
```

WithObservability attaches OpenTelemetry providers to Open. The opened dataset, and every handle derived from it, is instrumented with them.

<a name="WithSession"></a>
### func [WithSession](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L168>)

```go
func WithSession(s *Session) OpenOption
```

WithSession opens a dataset sharing s's index and metadata caches. It composes with the other Open options, including WithObjectStoreCache: Open\(ctx, uri, WithSession\(s\), WithObjectStoreCache\(c\)\) opens the dataset with a shared session AND a byte cache on the same handle.

<a name="WithStorageOptions"></a>
### func [WithStorageOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L141>)

```go
func WithStorageOptions(options map[string]string) OpenOption
```

WithStorageOptions passes object\-store configuration key/value pairs \(credentials, endpoints, ...\) to the underlying storage layer.

<a name="WithTag"></a>
### func [WithTag](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L151>)

```go
func WithTag(tag string) OpenOption
```

WithTag opens the dataset at the version referenced by a tag.

<a name="WithVersion"></a>
### func [WithVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L146>)

```go
func WithVersion(version uint64) OpenOption
```

WithVersion opens the dataset at a specific historical version.

<a name="Operation"></a>
## type [Operation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L630-L632>)

Operation is a dataset operation that can be constructed on the Go side and committed with CommitOperation. Implementations: UpdateConfigOperation, RestoreOperation.

```go
type Operation interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="OperationView"></a>
## type [OperationView](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L52-L69>)

OperationView is a typed summary of a transaction's operation. Type names the variant \("Append", "Delete", "UpdateConfig", ...\). The remaining fields are populated for the variants that carry them. Raw holds the operation's full JSON for anything not surfaced here.

```go
type OperationView struct {
    // Type names the operation variant ("Append", "Delete", "UpdateConfig", ...).
    Type string `json:"type"`
    // NumFragments is the number of fragments the operation touched, for
    // variants that carry it.
    NumFragments int `json:"num_fragments"`
    // FragmentIDs lists the fragment ids the operation touched, for variants
    // that carry them.
    FragmentIDs []uint64 `json:"fragment_ids"`
    // Predicate is the SQL predicate, for Delete-like variants.
    Predicate string `json:"predicate"`
    // Version is the target version, for Restore-like variants.
    Version uint64 `json:"version"`
    // NewIndices lists index names created by the operation, if any.
    NewIndices []string `json:"new_indices"`

    Raw json.RawMessage `json:"-"`
}
```

<a name="OperationView.UnmarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*OperationView\) [UnmarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L72>)

```go
func (o *OperationView) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error
```

UnmarshalJSON captures the operation's raw JSON alongside the typed fields.

<a name="OptimizeOption"></a>
## type [OptimizeOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L807>)

OptimizeOption configures OptimizeIndices.

```go
type OptimizeOption func(*optimizeConfig)
```

<a name="WithIndexNames"></a>
### func [WithIndexNames](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L816>)

```go
func WithIndexNames(names ...string) OptimizeOption
```

WithIndexNames restricts optimization to the named indices \(default all\).

<a name="WithMergeIndices"></a>
### func [WithMergeIndices](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L811>)

```go
func WithMergeIndices(n int) OptimizeOption
```

WithMergeIndices merges the delta updates and the latest n indices into a single index \(default 1: merge deltas into the most recent index\).

<a name="WithOptimizeTransactionProperties"></a>
### func [WithOptimizeTransactionProperties](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L829>)

```go
func WithOptimizeTransactionProperties(props map[string]string) OptimizeOption
```

WithOptimizeTransactionProperties attaches key\-value properties to the commit that optimization produces \(stored in the transaction file, e.g. a job id\).

<a name="WithRetrain"></a>
### func [WithRetrain](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L822>)

```go
func WithRetrain() OptimizeOption
```

WithRetrain retrains the whole index on the current data instead of merging deltas \(v3 vector indices only\).

PhraseQuery matches rows whose column contains the terms as a contiguous phrase. The inverted index must be built with position information \(Inverted\{WithPosition: true\}\).

```go
}
```

<a name="SQLBuilder"></a>
## type [SQLBuilder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L21-L25>)

SQLBuilder configures a SQL query over a dataset. Build one with Dataset.SQL, chain the configuration methods, then call Reader.

The underlying engine is DataFusion, and the dataset is registered as a table named "dataset" unless overridden with TableName.

```go
type SQLBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="SQLBuilder.All"></a>
### func \(\*SQLBuilder\) [All](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L83>)

```go
func (b *SQLBuilder) All(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

All executes the SQL query and returns an iterator over the result batches, as a range\-over\-func alternative to Reader.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the query, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="SQLBuilder.Reader"></a>
### func \(\*SQLBuilder\) [Reader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L69>)

```go
func (b *SQLBuilder) Reader(ctx context.Context) (reader array.RecordReader, err error)
```

Reader executes the query and returns a reader over the result batches.

The returned reader and every record obtained from it must be Released by the caller. Records returned by Next/RecordBatch are only valid until the next call to Next. Retain them to keep them longer. The reader stays valid after the Dataset is closed.

<a name="SQLBuilder.TableName"></a>
### func \(\*SQLBuilder\) [TableName](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L46>)

```go
func (b *SQLBuilder) TableName(name string) *SQLBuilder
```

TableName sets the table name the dataset is registered under in the SQL context \(default "dataset"\).

<a name="SQLBuilder.WithRowAddr"></a>
### func \(\*SQLBuilder\) [WithRowAddr](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L58>)

```go
func (b *SQLBuilder) WithRowAddr() *SQLBuilder
```

WithRowAddr exposes the internal \_rowaddr column to the query.

<a name="SQLBuilder.WithRowID"></a>
### func \(\*SQLBuilder\) [WithRowID](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/sql.go#L52>)

```go
func (b *SQLBuilder) WithRowID() *SQLBuilder
```

WithRowID exposes the internal \_rowid column to the query.

<a name="ScanStats"></a>
## type [ScanStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/callbacks.go#L172-L192>)

ScanStats reports summary I/O statistics for one scan execution, delivered to the callback registered with Scanner.WithScanStats. It mirrors the public fields of lance's ExecutionSummaryCounts \(rust/src/scanner.rs\). AllCounts / AllTimes are additional debugging metrics whose keys are subject to change upstream; treat them as opaque.

```go
type ScanStats struct {
    // IOPS is the number of I/O operations performed.
    IOPS uint64 `json:"iops"`
    // Requests is the number of requests made to the storage layer (may
    // differ from IOPS depending on coalescing configuration).
    Requests uint64 `json:"requests"`
    // BytesRead is the number of bytes read during plan execution.
    BytesRead uint64 `json:"bytes_read"`
    // IndicesLoaded is the number of top-level indices loaded.
    IndicesLoaded uint64 `json:"indices_loaded"`
    // PartsLoaded is the number of index partitions loaded.
    PartsLoaded uint64 `json:"parts_loaded"`
    // IndexComparisons is the number of index comparisons performed (the
    // exact meaning depends on the index type).
    IndexComparisons uint64 `json:"index_comparisons"`
    // AllCounts holds additional, unstable count metrics for debugging.
    AllCounts map[string]uint64 `json:"all_counts"`
    // AllTimes holds additional, unstable time metrics (nanoseconds) for
    // debugging.
    AllTimes map[string]uint64 `json:"all_times"`
}
```

<a name="Scanner"></a>
## type [Scanner](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L28-L39>)

Scanner is a builder for reads over a dataset. Configure it with the chained methods, then call one of the terminal methods \(Reader, CountRows, Explain\). A Scanner is cheap. Each terminal call snapshots the dataset, so the Scanner itself holds no native resources.

A Scanner must not be used concurrently while it is being configured, but the terminal methods are safe to call repeatedly.

```go
type Scanner struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Scanner.Aggregate"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Aggregate](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L260>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Aggregate(spec AggSpec) *Scanner
```

Aggregate aggregates the scan \(after any filter\). The aggregated result comes back through the normal terminals \(Reader, Batch\).

<a name="Scanner.All"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [All](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/iter.go#L68>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) All(ctx context.Context) iter.Seq2[arrow.RecordBatch, error]
```

All executes the scan and returns an iterator over the result batches, as a range\-over\-func alternative to Reader.

Each yielded batch is valid only for that iteration step: the underlying reader releases it as soon as the loop body returns \(advancing to the next batch\) or the sequence ends. Call Retain on a batch to keep it past its step, and Release it yourself when done. The underlying reader is always released when iteration ends, whether by exhaustion, an early break, or an error \- callers must not and need not call Release themselves. A failure opening the scan, or a mid\-stream error \(including context cancellation\), is yielded exactly once as \(nil, err\), after which the sequence ends.

<a name="Scanner.AnalyzeCountPlan"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [AnalyzeCountPlan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L787>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) AnalyzeCountPlan(ctx context.Context) (res string, err error)
```

AnalyzeCountPlan executes the COUNT\(\*\) form of the scan \(the plan CountRows runs\) and returns it annotated with runtime metrics.

<a name="Scanner.AnalyzePlan"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [AnalyzePlan](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L779>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) AnalyzePlan(ctx context.Context) (res string, err error)
```

AnalyzePlan executes the scan \(EXPLAIN ANALYZE\) and returns the execution plan annotated with runtime metrics. Note this runs the full scan.

<a name="Scanner.ApproxMode"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [ApproxMode](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L393>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) ApproxMode(mode string) *Scanner
```

ApproxMode sets the speed/accuracy tradeoff of a Nearest search on RQ\-quantized indexes \(other index types ignore it\): ApproxModeFast, ApproxModeNormal or ApproxModeAccurate. Only takes effect together with Nearest/NearestArrow.

<a name="Scanner.Batch"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Batch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L705>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Batch(ctx context.Context) (res arrow.RecordBatch, err error)
```

Batch executes the scan and returns all results combined into a single record. The caller must Release the record. Prefer Reader for large results: Batch materializes the whole result in memory.

<a name="Scanner.BatchReadahead"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [BatchReadahead](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L283>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) BatchReadahead(n int) *Scanner
```

BatchReadahead sets how many batches to read ahead \(legacy v1 data files only, ignored for v2\+\).

<a name="Scanner.BatchSize"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [BatchSize](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L157>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) BatchSize(n uint64) *Scanner
```

BatchSize caps the number of rows per emitted record batch.

<a name="Scanner.BatchSizeBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [BatchSizeBytes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L312>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) BatchSizeBytes(n uint64) *Scanner
```

BatchSizeBytes targets a batch size in bytes instead of rows, overriding BatchSize.

<a name="Scanner.BlobHandling"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [BlobHandling](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L411>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) BlobHandling(handling string) *Scanner
```

BlobHandling controls how blob columns are projected: BlobsAllBinary, BlobsDescriptions or BlobsAllDescriptions.

<a name="Scanner.Columns"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Columns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L120>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Columns(columns ...string) *Scanner
```

Columns restricts the scan to the given columns \(projection\). By default all columns are returned.

<a name="Scanner.CountRows"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [CountRows](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L684>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) CountRows(ctx context.Context) (n uint64, err error)
```

CountRows returns the number of rows the scan would produce, respecting the configured filter.

<a name="Scanner.DisableScoringAutoprojection"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [DisableScoringAutoprojection](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L419>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) DisableScoringAutoprojection() *Scanner
```

DisableScoringAutoprojection stops the \_score/\_distance column from being added automatically when an explicit projection is set. Include it in the projection to keep it.

<a name="Scanner.DistanceRange"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [DistanceRange](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L268>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) DistanceRange(lower, upper *float32) *Scanner
```

DistanceRange keeps only Nearest results whose \_distance is at least lower \(inclusive\) and less than upper \(exclusive\). Either bound may be nil for unbounded. Only takes effect together with Nearest/NearestArrow.

<a name="Scanner.Ef"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Ef](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L514>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Ef(ef int) *Scanner
```

Ef sets the HNSW search list size.

<a name="Scanner.Explain"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Explain](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L740>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Explain(ctx context.Context, verbose bool) (res string, err error)
```

<a name="Scanner.FastSearch"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [FastSearch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L528>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) FastSearch() *Scanner
```

FastSearch searches only indexed data, skipping rows not yet covered by the vector index.

<a name="Scanner.Filter"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Filter](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L126>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Filter(filter string) *Scanner
```

Filter applies an SQL predicate, e.g. "id \< 50".

<a name="Scanner.FilterSubstrait"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [FilterSubstrait](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L187>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) FilterSubstrait(expr []byte) *Scanner
```

<a name="Scanner.FragmentReadahead"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [FragmentReadahead](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L290>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) FragmentReadahead(n int) *Scanner
```

FragmentReadahead sets how many fragments to read ahead \(only used when ScanInOrder\(false\)\).

<a name="Scanner.FullTextSearch"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [FullTextSearch](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L536>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) FullTextSearch(q FtsQuery, opts ...FtsOption) *Scanner
```

FullTextSearch configures a full\-text search over one or more inverted\- indexed columns. Results gain a "\_score" column and are ordered by descending relevance.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleScanner\_FullTextSearch builds an inverted \(full\-text\) index over a text column and runs a MatchQuery against it. Results are ordered by descending BM25 relevance, so the document matching both query terms \("database" and "search"\) ranks above the one matching only "search".

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow"
	"github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/array"

	"github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/lance"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "lance-example-fts-*")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir)
	uri := filepath.Join(dir, "example.lance")

	docs := []string{
		"the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog",
		"lance is a columnar data format built for machine learning",
		"a vector database stores embeddings for similarity search",
		"full text search uses an inverted index over tokens",
	}

	schema := arrow.NewSchema([]arrow.Field{
		{Name: "id", Type: arrow.PrimitiveTypes.Int64},
		{Name: "text", Type: arrow.BinaryTypes.String},
	}, nil)
	b := array.NewRecordBuilder(lance.Allocator(), schema)
	defer b.Release()
	idB := b.Field(0).(*array.Int64Builder)
	textB := b.Field(1).(*array.StringBuilder)
	for i, doc := range docs {
		idB.Append(int64(i))
		textB.Append(doc)
	}
	rec := b.NewRecordBatch()
	defer rec.Release()
	rdr, err := array.NewRecordReader(schema, []arrow.RecordBatch{rec})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	ds, err := lance.Write(ctx, uri, rdr)
	rdr.Release()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer ds.Close()

	if err := ds.CreateIndex(ctx, "text", lance.Inverted{}, lance.WithIndexName("text_idx")); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	res, err := ds.Scan().
		FullTextSearch(lance.MatchQuery{Column: "text", Terms: "database search"}).
		Reader(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer res.Release()
	for res.Next() {
		batch := res.RecordBatch()
		ids := batch.Column(batch.Schema().FieldIndices("id")[0]).(*array.Int64)
		for i := 0; i < int(batch.NumRows()); i++ {
			fmt.Println(ids.Value(i))
		}
	}
	if err := res.Err(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
2
3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Scanner.IOBufferSize"></a>

```go

```

IOBufferSize sets the amount of RAM \(bytes\) reserved for buffered I/O \(default 2 GiB\).


<a name="Scanner.IndexSegments"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [IndexSegments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L374>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) IndexSegments(uuids ...string) *Scanner
```

IndexSegments restricts a Nearest search to the given vector index segment UUIDs.

<a name="Scanner.Limit"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Limit](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L139>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Limit(limit int64) *Scanner
```

Limit caps the number of rows returned.

### func \(\*Scanner\) [MaterializationStyle](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L360>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) MaterializationStyle(style string) *Scanner
```

MaterializationStyle controls when non\-filter columns are fetched from storage: MaterializationHeuristic, MaterializationAllLate or MaterializationAllEarly.

<a name="Scanner.MaximumNprobes"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [MaximumNprobes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L502>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) MaximumNprobes(n int) *Scanner
```

MaximumNprobes sets the maximum number of IVF partitions to probe.

<a name="Scanner.Metric"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Metric](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L483>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Metric(metric DistanceType) *Scanner
```

Metric overrides the distance metric for a Nearest search \(default: the metric the vector index was built with, or L2 without an index\).

<a name="Scanner.MinimumNprobes"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [MinimumNprobes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L496>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) MinimumNprobes(n int) *Scanner
```

MinimumNprobes sets the minimum number of IVF partitions to probe.

<a name="Scanner.Nearest"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Nearest](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L455>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Nearest(column string, vector []float32, k int) *Scanner
```

Nearest configures a k\-nearest\-neighbor search on a vector column. The query vector's length must match the column dimension \(or a multiple of it, for batch queries against fixed\-size\-list columns\). Combine with Filter and Prefilter\(true\) to restrict the searched rows. Results gain a "\_distance" column and are ordered by ascending distance.

<a name="Scanner.NearestArrow"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [NearestArrow](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L473>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) NearestArrow(column string, vector arrow.Array, k int) *Scanner
```

NearestArrow is Nearest with an arbitrary Arrow query array \(a Float16/Float32/Float64/UInt8 values array, or a \(FixedSize\)List of those for multivector/batch queries\).

The Scanner borrows the array: the caller must keep it valid \(not Released\) until the terminal methods it wants to run have returned. The array's buffers are exported across the Arrow C Data Interface on each terminal call, so they must live outside the Go heap \(allocate with a C\-backed allocator returned by Allocator, enforced under GOEXPERIMENT=cgocheck2\).

<a name="Scanner.Nprobes"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Nprobes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L490>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Nprobes(n int) *Scanner
```

Nprobes sets the exact number of IVF partitions to probe \(both the minimum and the maximum\).

<a name="Scanner.Offset"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Offset](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L145>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Offset(offset int64) *Scanner
```

Offset skips the first offset rows.

<a name="Scanner.OrderBy"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [OrderBy](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L204>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) OrderBy(column string, opts ...OrderOption) *Scanner
```

OrderBy sorts the results by column, ascending. Repeated OrderBy / OrderByDesc calls add secondary sort columns. Sorting requires reading all data before the first batch is returned.

<a name="Scanner.OrderByDesc"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [OrderByDesc](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L209>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) OrderByDesc(column string, opts ...OrderOption) *Scanner
```

OrderByDesc sorts the results by column, descending. See OrderBy.

<a name="Scanner.Prefilter"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Prefilter](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L133>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Prefilter(prefilter bool) *Scanner
```

Prefilter controls whether the filter runs before \(true\) or after any vector search stage.

<a name="Scanner.ProjectExpr"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [ProjectExpr](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L179>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) ProjectExpr(name, expr string) *Scanner
```

ProjectExpr adds a computed output column: name is the output column name and expr an SQL expression evaluated per row \(e.g. "id \* 2"\). Repeatable. The projection then consists of exactly the ProjectExpr columns. Mutually exclusive with Columns.

<a name="Scanner.QueryParallelism"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [QueryParallelism](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L305>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) QueryParallelism(n int32) *Scanner
```

QueryParallelism sets the partition\-search concurrency of a Nearest query: 0 automatic \(default\), \-1 the CPU pool size, 1 sequential, \>= 2 partition\-parallel. Only takes effect together with Nearest/NearestArrow.

<a name="Scanner.Reader"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Reader](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L660>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Reader(ctx context.Context) (reader array.RecordReader, err error)
```

Reader executes the scan and returns a reader over the result batches.

The returned reader and every record obtained from it must be Released by the caller. Records returned by Next/RecordBatch are only valid until the next call to Next. Retain them to keep them longer. The reader stays valid after the Dataset is closed.

<a name="Scanner.Refine"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [Refine](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L508>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) Refine(factor uint32) *Scanner
```

Refine re\-ranks factor\*k approximate candidates with exact distances.

<a name="Scanner.ScanFragments"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [ScanFragments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L367>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) ScanFragments(ids ...uint32) *Scanner
```

ScanFragments restricts the scan to the given fragment ids \(see Version/fragment metadata for ids\). Unknown ids fail with ErrNotFound.

<a name="Scanner.ScanInOrder"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [ScanInOrder](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L164>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) ScanInOrder(ordered bool) *Scanner
```

ScanInOrder controls whether batches are returned in stable row order \(the default\) or in whatever order fragments finish reading.

<a name="Scanner.StrictBatchSize"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [StrictBatchSize](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L319>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) StrictBatchSize(strict bool) *Scanner
```

StrictBatchSize forces every emitted batch \(except the last\) to have exactly BatchSize rows, at the cost of a data copy.

<a name="Scanner.TargetParallelism"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [TargetParallelism](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L297>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) TargetParallelism(n int) *Scanner
```

TargetParallelism overrides the target number of partitions used by the physical query optimizer \(default: the number of compute CPUs\).

<a name="Scanner.UseIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [UseIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L521>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) UseIndex(use bool) *Scanner
```

UseIndex controls whether a Nearest search may use a vector index \(default true\). Pass false to force a flat \(exact\) search.

<a name="Scanner.UseScalarIndex"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [UseScalarIndex](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L341>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) UseScalarIndex(use bool) *Scanner
```

UseScalarIndex controls whether scalar indices may be used to optimize the query \(default true\).

<a name="Scanner.UseStats"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [UseStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L334>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) UseStats(use bool) *Scanner
```

UseStats controls whether statistics are used to optimize the scan \(default true\), mainly for debugging and benchmarking.

<a name="Scanner.WithRowAddress"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [WithRowAddress](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L170>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) WithRowAddress() *Scanner
```

WithRowAddress includes the internal \_rowaddr column in the results.

<a name="Scanner.WithRowID"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [WithRowID](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L151>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) WithRowID() *Scanner
```

WithRowID includes the internal \_rowid column in the results.

<a name="Scanner.WithScanStats"></a>
### func \(\*Scanner\) [WithScanStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/scanner.go#L445>)

```go
func (s *Scanner) WithScanStats(fn func(ScanStats)) *Scanner
```

WithScanStats registers fn to receive summary I/O statistics once after the scan executes. A nil fn clears any previously registered callback.

In lance 8.0.0, the report is delivered ONLY by the streaming terminals \(Reader, and therefore the Scanner.All iterator\) and by Batch. CountRows, Explain, AnalyzePlan, and AnalyzeCountPlan accept the option without error, but upstream does not propagate the callback on those code paths, so they deliver no report.

For Reader/All, fn fires on the consuming goroutine's thread when the stream is exhausted, while the reader's internal lock is held: fn must not touch that reader \(Next, RecordBatch, Release\), or it will deadlock. If the reader is released before exhaustion \(early break\), no stats are delivered. For Batch, fn fires before the call returns.

fn is fire\-and\-forget \(its return value, if any, is ignored and errors are swallowed\) and it MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(opening/scanning a dataset, CountRows, etc.\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall inside the native layer rather than crashing the process, but since reporting is best\-effort that failure is silently dropped and the scan still completes.

<a name="Session"></a>
## type [Session](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L24-L35>)

Session holds index and metadata caches shared across dataset opens and writes. Reusing one Session across Open/Write calls lets a second open of the same dataset serve schema, manifest and index data from cache instead of re\-reading storage. A Session is safe for concurrent use. Close releases it \(idempotent\).

```go
type Session struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSession"></a>
### func [NewSession](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L87>)

```go
func NewSession(cfg SessionConfig, opts ...ObsOption) (*Session, error)
```

NewSession creates a Session with in\-memory index and metadata caches of the configured byte budgets. Close it when done.

<a name="NewSessionWithCacheBackend"></a>
### func [NewSessionWithCacheBackend](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L108>)

```go
func NewSessionWithCacheBackend(b CacheBackend, metadataCacheBytes uint64, opts ...ObsOption) (*Session, error)
```

NewSessionWithCacheBackend creates a Session whose index cache is backed by b. Only serializable, codec\-bearing index entries \(IVF/PQ/RQ partitions, FTS posting lists, scalar\-index states, ...\) are delegated to b. Non\- serializable "live object" entries stay in an in\-process cache, and the metadata cache stays in\-memory \(Lance 8.0 has no metadata\-cache backend hook\). Close releases this Session handle; native datasets opened through it retain the backend registration until their final clone is released.

<a name="Session.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Session\) [Close](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L153>)

```go
func (s *Session) Close() error
```

Close releases this session handle. A cache\-backend registration remains alive while datasets opened with the session still hold native Session clones, and is released when the last clone is dropped. Close is idempotent.

<a name="Session.Open"></a>
### func \(\*Session\) [Open](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L183>)

```go
func (s *Session) Open(ctx context.Context, uri string, opts ...OpenOption) (*Dataset, error)
```

Open opens a dataset sharing this session's caches. It accepts the same OpenOptions as the package\-level Open \(WithVersion/WithTag/ WithStorageOptions/WithObjectStoreCache\). The session owns the cache sizes. It is a thin wrapper over Open\(ctx, uri, append\(opts, WithSession\(s\)\)...\).

<a name="Session.Stats"></a>
### func \(\*Session\) [Stats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L130>)

```go
func (s *Session) Stats() (SessionStats, error)
```

Stats returns a snapshot of the session's cache statistics.

<a name="Session.Write"></a>
### func \(\*Session\) [Write](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L191>)

```go
func (s *Session) Write(ctx context.Context, uri string, rdr array.RecordReader, opts ...WriteOption) (*Dataset, error)
```

Write writes rdr to a dataset at uri with this session's caches attached. It accepts every package\-level WriteOption, including blob, multi\-base, manifest, auto\-cleanup, and transaction\-property options. It is a thin wrapper over Write\(ctx, uri, rdr, append\(opts, WithWriteSession\(s\)\)...\).

<a name="Session.WriteWithProgress"></a>
### func \(\*Session\) [WriteWithProgress](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L203>)

```go
func (s *Session) WriteWithProgress(ctx context.Context, uri string, rdr array.RecordReader, progress func(WriteStats), opts ...WriteOption) (*Dataset, error)
```

WriteWithProgress is like Session.Write but also reports cumulative WriteStats to progress after each batch is written. It is a thin wrapper over Write with WithWriteSession and WithWriteProgress.

progress runs synchronously on the write path and MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(opening/scanning a dataset, CountRows, etc.\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall rather than crashing the process. Because progress is best\-effort, that error is ignored and the write still completes.

<a name="SessionConfig"></a>
## type [SessionConfig](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L53-L58>)

SessionConfig configures the in\-memory caches of a Session.

```go
type SessionConfig struct {
    // IndexCacheBytes is the byte budget of the index cache.
    IndexCacheBytes uint64
    // MetadataCacheBytes is the byte budget of the metadata cache.
    MetadataCacheBytes uint64
}
```

<a name="SessionStats"></a>
## type [SessionStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L73-L83>)

SessionStats reports a snapshot of a Session's cache statistics.

```go
type SessionStats struct {
    // IndexCache reports the index cache's hit/miss and size counters.
    IndexCache CacheCounts `json:"index_cache"`
    // MetadataCache reports the metadata cache's hit/miss and size counters.
    MetadataCache CacheCounts `json:"metadata_cache"`
    // SizeBytes is the approximate total memory held by both caches.
    SizeBytes uint64 `json:"size_bytes"`
    // ApproxNumItems is the approximate total number of entries across both
    // caches.
    ApproxNumItems uint64 `json:"approx_num_items"`
}
```

<a name="SourceDedupeBehavior"></a>
## type [SourceDedupeBehavior](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L171>)

SourceDedupeBehavior controls how a merge\-insert handles duplicate source rows that match the same target row on the join key.

```go
type SourceDedupeBehavior string
```

<a name="SourceDedupeFail"></a>

```go
const (
    // SourceDedupeFail fails the operation when the source contains duplicate
    // keys that match the same target row. This is Lance's default.
    SourceDedupeFail SourceDedupeBehavior = "fail"
    // SourceDedupeFirstSeen keeps the first-encountered source row for a given
    // key and skips subsequent duplicates (counted in
    // MergeStats.NumSkippedDuplicates).
    SourceDedupeFirstSeen SourceDedupeBehavior = "first_seen"
)
```

<a name="TagContents"></a>
## type [TagContents](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/version.go#L226-L240>)

TagContents is the full detail of a single tag, as returned by Tags.Get \(the same per\-tag shape List returns for every tag, plus timestamps\).

```go
type TagContents struct {
    // Version is the version number the tag points at.
    Version uint64 `json:"version"`
    // Branch is the branch the version belongs to, or "" for the main
    // branch.
    Branch string `json:"branch"`
    // CreatedAt is when the tag was created, RFC3339, if reported.
    CreatedAt string `json:"createdAt,omitempty"`
    // UpdatedAt is when the tag was last moved, RFC3339, if reported.
    UpdatedAt string `json:"updatedAt,omitempty"`
    // ManifestSize is the size in bytes of the tagged version's manifest.
    ManifestSize uint64 `json:"manifestSize"`
    // Metadata holds key-value pairs attached to the tag.
    Metadata map[string]string `json:"metadata"`
}
```

Tags manages the tags of a dataset. Obtain one with Dataset.Tags. It is only valid while the originating Dataset is open.

```go
type Tags struct {

}
```



<a name="TakeSpec"></a>
## type [TakeSpec](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/take.go#L28-L47>)

TakeSpec describes a point read. Exactly one of Indices, RowIDs or Addresses must be set, at most one of Columns or SQLProjection.

```go
type TakeSpec struct {
    // Indices are row offsets in the dataset (0 = first live row. Deleted
    // rows are skipped).
    Indices []uint64
    // RowIDs are stable row ids (write the dataset with
    // WithStableRowIDs(true) for ids that survive compaction. Without the
    // feature, row ids equal row addresses).
    RowIDs []uint64
    // Addresses are row addresses (fragment_id << 32 | row_offset), e.g.
    // obtained from a scan with WithRowID or a take with WithRowAddress.
    Addresses []uint64
    // Columns restricts the result to the given columns. Empty means all
    // columns.
    Columns []string
    // SQLProjection computes the result columns with SQL expressions
    // instead of a plain column list.
    SQLProjection []NamedExpr
    // WithRowAddress includes the internal _rowaddr column in the result.
    WithRowAddress bool
}
```

<a name="Transaction"></a>
## type [Transaction](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L31-L38>)

Transaction is an uncommitted change to a dataset, produced by WriteFragments \(or read back with Dataset.ReadTransaction\). It carries two representations:

- a lossless protobuf encoding \(the exact form Lance persists\), used to commit the transaction and to round\-trip variants this package does not model, the true escape hatch, held opaquely, and
- Raw, a JSON summary of the transaction for inspection, plus typed accessors decoded from it.

Lance's Transaction/Operation types are protobuf\-backed \(not JSON\), so the protobuf bytes, not the JSON view, are what survive a commit losslessly.

```go
type Transaction struct {

    // Raw is the full JSON summary of the transaction (escape hatch for
    // fields the typed accessors do not surface).
    Raw json.RawMessage
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="WriteFragments"></a>
### func [WriteFragments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L199>)

```go
func WriteFragments(ctx context.Context, uri string, rdr array.RecordReader, opts ...WriteFragmentsOption) (res *Transaction, err error)
```

WriteFragments writes rdr's batches as new, uncommitted fragments at uri and returns the resulting Transaction. Nothing is committed: ship the returned Transaction to a driver and commit it with NewCommit\(...\).Execute / ExecuteBatch. This is the primitive for distributed writes. Run it on N workers over disjoint data.

As with Write, rdr's batches are exported across the Arrow C Data Interface and must be C\-allocated with Allocator.

<a name="Transaction.Bytes"></a>
### func \(\*Transaction\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L92>)

```go
func (t *Transaction) Bytes() []byte
```

Bytes returns a copy of the opaque, lossless protobuf encoding of the transaction.

<a name="Transaction.Operation"></a>
### func \(\*Transaction\) [Operation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L85>)

```go
func (t *Transaction) Operation() OperationView
```

Operation returns a typed summary of the transaction's operation.

```go
func (t *Transaction) Properties() map[string]string
```

Properties returns a copy of the transaction's properties, if any.

```go
func (t *Transaction) ReadVersion() uint64
```

ReadVersion is the dataset version the transaction is based on.

<a name="Transaction.UUID"></a>

```go
func (t *Transaction) UUID() string
```

UUID is the transaction's unique id.

## type [TransactionInfo](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/delta.go#L40-L51>)

TransactionInfo summarizes one transaction between two dataset versions.

```go
type TransactionInfo struct {
    // ReadVersion is the version the transaction was based on.
    ReadVersion uint64 `json:"read_version"`
    // UUID uniquely identifies the transaction.
    UUID string `json:"uuid"`
    // Operation is the operation kind, e.g. "Append", "Delete", "Update".
    Operation string `json:"operation"`
    // Tag is the transaction's tag, if any.
    Tag string `json:"tag"`
    // Properties holds the transaction properties recorded at write time.
    Properties map[string]string `json:"properties"`
}
```

```go
func (s *TransactionSummary) UUID() string
```

<a name="UDFCheckpointStore"></a>
## type [UDFCheckpointStore](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L80-L92>)

UDFCheckpointStore persists intermediate AddColumnsUDF results so an interrupted run can resume without recomputing. It is optional \(see WithUDFCheckpoint\). Fragments cross the boundary as opaque JSON bytes. Treat them as opaque tokens keyed by fragment id. Batches passed to InsertBatch are only valid during the call. Retain or copy to keep them. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.

Store methods run synchronously inside the native add\-columns operation and MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(opening/scanning a dataset, CountRows, etc.\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall rather than crashing the process, and that error surfaces as a failure of the enclosing operation.

```go
type UDFCheckpointStore interface {
    // GetBatch returns a previously checkpointed batch for (fragmentID,
    // batchIndex), if present.
    GetBatch(fragmentID uint32, batchIndex int) (batch arrow.RecordBatch, found bool, err error)
    // InsertBatch checkpoints batch for (fragmentID, batchIndex).
    InsertBatch(fragmentID uint32, batchIndex int, batch arrow.RecordBatch) error
    // GetFragment returns the opaque fragment JSON previously stored for
    // fragmentID, if present.
    GetFragment(fragmentID uint32) (fragmentJSON []byte, found bool, err error)
    // InsertFragment stores the opaque fragment JSON. The fragment id is
    // embedded in the JSON.
    InsertFragment(fragmentJSON []byte) error
}
```

<a name="UDFOption"></a>
## type [UDFOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L102>)

UDFOption configures AddColumnsUDF.

```go
type UDFOption func(*udfOptions)
```

<a name="WithUDFBatchSize"></a>
### func [WithUDFBatchSize](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L112>)

```go
func WithUDFBatchSize(n uint32) UDFOption
```

WithUDFBatchSize sets the number of rows per mapper batch \(default: engine default\).

<a name="WithUDFCheckpoint"></a>
### func [WithUDFCheckpoint](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L118>)

```go
func WithUDFCheckpoint(store UDFCheckpointStore) UDFOption
```

WithUDFCheckpoint attaches a checkpoint store consulted before recomputing each batch/fragment.

<a name="WithUDFReadColumns"></a>
### func [WithUDFReadColumns](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L106>)

```go
func WithUDFReadColumns(columns ...string) UDFOption
```

WithUDFReadColumns restricts the input batches passed to the mapper to the named columns \(default: all columns\).

<a name="UncommittedOption"></a>
## type [UncommittedOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L97>)

UncommittedOption configures CreateIndexUncommitted.

```go
type UncommittedOption func(*createUncommittedConfig)
```

<a name="WithFragments"></a>
### func [WithFragments](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L114>)

```go
func WithFragments(ids ...uint32) UncommittedOption
```

WithFragments restricts the build to the given fragment ids \(a worker's slice\).

A variadic cannot tell "no fragments" from "not called", so calling WithFragments with no ids \(or spreading a nil slice\) is rejected with ErrInvalidArgument rather than silently meaning "all fragments". To restrict the build to zero fragments \(an empty worker assignment\), spread an explicit empty slice: WithFragments\(emptySlice...\) where emptySlice is \[\]uint32\{\}.

<a name="WithIndexUUID"></a>
### func [WithIndexUUID](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L129>)

```go
func WithIndexUUID(uuid string) UncommittedOption
```

WithIndexUUID pins the segment's index UUID.

<a name="WithTrain"></a>
### func [WithTrain](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L102>)

```go
func WithTrain(train bool) UncommittedOption
```

WithTrain controls whether the IVF/quantizer model is trained. Set false when supplying shared centroids \(via the IndexConfig's VectorOptions.Centroids\) so every distributed segment stays comparable.

<a name="WithUncommittedName"></a>
### func [WithUncommittedName](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/distributed.go#L134>)

```go
func WithUncommittedName(name string) UncommittedOption
```

WithUncommittedName sets the index name \(defaults to "\<column\>\_idx"\).

```go
func WithUncommittedTransactionProperties(props map[string]string) UncommittedOption
```

WithUncommittedTransactionProperties attaches properties recorded with the eventual commit.

<a name="UpdateConfigOperation"></a>
## type [UpdateConfigOperation](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L637-L642>)

UpdateConfigOperation upserts and/or deletes dataset config, table\-metadata, or schema\-metadata entries. A key mapped to nil in a \*Deletes map is removed. Entries in a \*Upserts map are set.

```go
type UpdateConfigOperation struct {
    ConfigUpserts         map[string]string
    ConfigDeletes         []string
    TableMetadataUpserts  map[string]string
    SchemaMetadataUpserts map[string]string
}
```

<a name="UpdateResult"></a>
## type [UpdateResult](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L32-L35>)

UpdateResult reports the outcome of an Update.

```go
type UpdateResult struct {
    // RowsUpdated is the number of rows modified.
    RowsUpdated uint64 `json:"rows_updated"`
}
```

<a name="UpdateSpec"></a>
## type [UpdateSpec](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/mutation.go#L38-L51>)

UpdateSpec describes an Update operation.

```go
type UpdateSpec struct {
    // Set maps column names to SQL expressions producing the new value,
    // e.g. {"score": "score * 2"}. It must contain at least one entry.
    Set map[string]string
    // Where restricts the update to rows matching this SQL predicate.
    // Empty means "update every row".
    Where string
    // ConflictRetries overrides the number of times the operation is
    // retried on commit contention (nil keeps the Lance default of 10).
    ConflictRetries *uint32
    // RetryTimeout caps the total time spent on retries (zero keeps the
    // Lance default of 30 seconds).
    RetryTimeout time.Duration
}
```

<a name="VectorOptions"></a>
## type [VectorOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L319-L364>)

VectorOptions holds advanced build knobs shared by every vector index type. Embedded \(zero\-valued\) in each vector config. Every field's zero value means "use the Lance default". Set fields via the embedded struct, e.g. lance.IvfPq\{VectorOptions: lance.VectorOptions\{TargetPartitionSize: 500\}\}.

```go
type VectorOptions struct {
    // TargetPartitionSize derives the IVF partition count from a target
    // per-partition size, preferred over Partitions when set (>0).
    TargetPartitionSize uint
    // Retrain retrains the provided Centroids instead of using them verbatim.
    Retrain bool
    // StreamingSampleRate sets the per-step sample rate for streaming IVF
    // k-means training (>0 enables streaming training).
    StreamingSampleRate uint
    // StreamingCoresetRate sets the coreset rate for streaming IVF training.
    StreamingCoresetRate uint
    // StreamingRefinePasses adds extra streaming Lloyd refinement passes.
    StreamingRefinePasses uint
    // ShufflePartitionBatches controls shuffle batches per partition
    // (advanced).
    ShufflePartitionBatches uint
    // ShufflePartitionConcurrency controls concurrent shuffle partitions
    // (advanced).
    ShufflePartitionConcurrency uint
    // PrecomputedPartitionsFile is a precomputed row_id -> partition_id file.
    PrecomputedPartitionsFile string
    // StorageOptions configures the object store used to load precomputed
    // partitions.
    StorageOptions map[string]string
    // KmeansRedos runs PQ k-means this many times and keeps the best result
    // (PQ variants only, 0 = default 1).
    KmeansRedos uint
    // PrefetchDistance sets the HNSW graph-build prefetch distance
    // (IVF_HNSW_* variants only).
    PrefetchDistance uint
    // IndexFileVersion selects the index file format: "legacy" or "v3"
    // (empty = the index type's default).
    IndexFileVersion string
    // SkipTranspose skips transpose/packing for PQ and RQ storage when set.
    SkipTranspose *bool
    // RuntimeHints are optional build preferences stored in the manifest
    // (reverse-DNS keys, e.g. "lance.ivf.max_iters").
    RuntimeHints map[string]string
    // Centroids is an optional pre-computed IVF centroids array
    // (FixedSizeList of the column's vector type/dim). Passed across the
    // Arrow C Data Interface.
    Centroids arrow.Array
    // Codebook is an optional pre-computed PQ codebook array (PQ variants
    // only). Passed across the Arrow C Data Interface.
    Codebook arrow.Array
}
```

<a name="WithFragmentsTransactionProperties"></a>
### func [WithFragmentsTransactionProperties](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/transaction.go#L187>)

```go
func WithFragmentsTransactionProperties(props map[string]string) WriteFragmentsOption
```

WithFragmentsTransactionProperties attaches key/value properties to the resulting Transaction. The properties travel inside the transaction itself \(they survive the commit and are readable via Dataset.ReadTransaction\).

<a name="WriteMode"></a>
## type [WriteMode](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L74>)

WriteMode controls how Write behaves when the destination dataset exists.

```go
type WriteMode int
```

<a name="WriteModeCreate"></a>

```go
const (
    // WriteModeCreate creates a new dataset and fails if one already exists.
    WriteModeCreate WriteMode = iota
    // WriteModeAppend appends to an existing dataset.
    WriteModeAppend
    // WriteModeOverwrite replaces the contents of an existing dataset as a
    // new version (older versions remain accessible via time travel).
    WriteModeOverwrite
)
```

<a name="WriteMode.String"></a>
### func \(WriteMode\) [String](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L88>)

```go
func (m WriteMode) String() string
```

String returns the mode's wire representation \("create", "append", or "overwrite"\).

<a name="WriteOption"></a>
## type [WriteOption](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L184>)

WriteOption configures Write. The concrete options are produced by the With\* constructors \(WithMode, WithWriteSession, WithWriteProgress, ...\).

```go
type WriteOption func(*writeConfig)
```

<a name="WithAllowExternalBlobOutsideBases"></a>
### func [WithAllowExternalBlobOutsideBases](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L119>)

```go
func WithAllowExternalBlobOutsideBases(allow bool) WriteOption
```

WithAllowExternalBlobOutsideBases permits writing external blob URIs that cannot be mapped to any registered non\-dataset\-root base path \(such rows are rejected by default\).

<a name="WithAutoCleanup"></a>
### func [WithAutoCleanup](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L81>)

```go
func WithAutoCleanup(interval uint64, olderThan time.Duration) WriteOption
```

WithAutoCleanup makes every interval\-th commit of a NEW dataset automatically clean up versions older than olderThan. It has no effect on existing datasets \(set the lance.auto\_cleanup.\* config keys instead\). Auto\-cleanup adds per\-commit latency that grows with the version count. Prefer explicit CleanupOldVersions calls when reclaiming space matters.

<a name="WithBaseStoreParams"></a>
### func [WithBaseStoreParams](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L147>)

```go
func WithBaseStoreParams(base string, options map[string]string) WriteOption
```

WithBaseStoreParams sets storage options \(credentials, endpoints, ...\) for a single registered base path, keyed by base name or path. The write\-level storage options remain the fallback for other bases. Call repeatedly for multiple bases.

<a name="WithBlobPackFileSizeThreshold"></a>
### func [WithBlobPackFileSizeThreshold](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L112>)

```go
func WithBlobPackFileSizeThreshold(bytes uint64) WriteOption
```

WithBlobPackFileSizeThreshold caps the size in bytes of blob v2 pack \(.blob\) sidecar files. When a pack file reaches the threshold a new one is started \(default 1 GiB\).

<a name="WithDataStorageVersion"></a>
### func [WithDataStorageVersion](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L208>)

```go
func WithDataStorageVersion(version string) WriteOption
```

WithDataStorageVersion selects the Lance file format version to write, e.g. "2.0", "2.1", "2.2" or "stable".

<a name="WithExternalBlobMode"></a>
### func [WithExternalBlobMode](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L105>)

```go
func WithExternalBlobMode(mode ExternalBlobMode) WriteOption
```

WithExternalBlobMode selects how external blob URIs are handled \(ExternalBlobReference, the default, or ExternalBlobIngest\).

<a name="WithInitialBases"></a>
### func [WithInitialBases](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L126>)

```go
func WithInitialBases(bases ...BasePath) WriteOption
```

WithInitialBases registers additional storage base paths in the manifest of a NEW dataset \(create/overwrite modes only\). Each base must have a unique non\-zero ID.

<a name="WithMaxBytesPerFile"></a>
### func [WithMaxBytesPerFile](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L202>)

```go
func WithMaxBytesPerFile(n uint64) WriteOption
```

WithMaxBytesPerFile caps the \(approximate\) size of a single data file.

<a name="WithMaxRowsPerFile"></a>
### func [WithMaxRowsPerFile](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L192>)

```go
func WithMaxRowsPerFile(n uint64) WriteOption
```

WithMaxRowsPerFile caps the number of rows written to a single data file.

<a name="WithMaxRowsPerGroup"></a>
### func [WithMaxRowsPerGroup](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L197>)

```go
func WithMaxRowsPerGroup(n uint64) WriteOption
```

WithMaxRowsPerGroup caps the number of rows in a single row group.

<a name="WithMode"></a>
### func [WithMode](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L187>)

```go
func WithMode(mode WriteMode) WriteOption
```

WithMode sets the write mode \(default WriteModeCreate\).

<a name="WithSkipAutoCleanup"></a>
### func [WithSkipAutoCleanup](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L92>)

```go
func WithSkipAutoCleanup(skip bool) WriteOption
```

WithSkipAutoCleanup skips auto\-cleanup during this write's commit \(useful for high\-frequency writers, or writers without delete permissions\).

<a name="WithStableRowIDs"></a>
### func [WithStableRowIDs](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L214>)

```go
func WithStableRowIDs(enable bool) WriteOption
```

WithStableRowIDs enables stable row IDs that survive compaction and updates.

<a name="WithTargetBaseNamesOrPaths"></a>
### func [WithTargetBaseNamesOrPaths](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L139>)

```go
func WithTargetBaseNamesOrPaths(namesOrPaths ...string) WriteOption
```

WithTargetBaseNamesOrPaths directs the write's new data files to the bases with the given names or paths \(resolved to IDs when the write executes\).

<a name="WithTargetBases"></a>
### func [WithTargetBases](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L132>)

```go
func WithTargetBases(ids ...uint32) WriteOption
```

WithTargetBases directs the write's new data files to the bases with the given IDs \(registered via WithInitialBases or Dataset.AddBases\).

<a name="WithTransactionProperties"></a>
### func [WithTransactionProperties](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L99>)

```go
func WithTransactionProperties(properties map[string]string) WriteOption
```

WithTransactionProperties attaches key\-value pairs \(commit messages, engine information, ...\) to the write's transaction. They are persisted as part of the transaction and readable via Delta\(\).Transactions.

<a name="WithV2ManifestPaths"></a>
### func [WithV2ManifestPaths](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/writeparams.go#L72>)

```go
func WithV2ManifestPaths(enable bool) WriteOption
```

WithV2ManifestPaths controls whether a NEW dataset uses the V2 manifest naming scheme \(constant\-time latest\-manifest lookups on object storage, default true\). It has no effect on existing datasets. Use Dataset.MigrateManifestPathsV2 to migrate those.

<a name="WithWriteObservability"></a>
### func [WithWriteObservability](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L262>)

```go
func WithWriteObservability(opts ...ObsOption) WriteOption
```

WithWriteObservability attaches OpenTelemetry providers to Write. The returned dataset, and every handle derived from it, is instrumented with them.

<a name="WithWriteProgress"></a>
### func [WithWriteProgress](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L47>)

```go
func WithWriteProgress(progress func(WriteStats)) WriteOption
```

WithWriteProgress reports cumulative WriteStats to progress after each batch is written. It composes with the other Write options, including WithWriteSession. A nil progress is a no\-op.

progress runs synchronously on the write path and MUST NOT re\-enter lance\-go \(opening/scanning a dataset, CountRows, etc.\). A re\-entrant call is rejected with ErrReentrantCall rather than crashing the process. Because progress is best\-effort, that error is ignored and the write still completes.

<a name="WithWriteSession"></a>
### func [WithWriteSession](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/session.go#L175>)

```go
func WithWriteSession(s *Session) WriteOption
```

WithWriteSession writes a dataset sharing s's index and metadata caches. It composes with the other Write options. This is the Write counterpart of WithSession.

<a name="WithWriteStorageOptions"></a>
### func [WithWriteStorageOptions](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/dataset.go#L220>)

```go
func WithWriteStorageOptions(options map[string]string) WriteOption
```

WithWriteStorageOptions passes object\-store configuration key/value pairs to the underlying storage layer for the write.

<a name="WriteStats"></a>
## type [WriteStats](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/hooks.go#L29-L36>)

WriteStats reports cumulative progress of a write, delivered to the callback registered with WriteWithProgress / Session.WriteWithProgress after each batch is written.

```go
type WriteStats struct {
    // BytesWritten is the cumulative number of data-file bytes written so far.
    BytesWritten uint64
    // RowsWritten is the cumulative number of rows written so far.
    RowsWritten uint64
    // FilesWritten is the cumulative number of data files written so far.
    FilesWritten uint32
}
```

<a name="ZoneMap"></a>
## type [ZoneMap](<https://github.com/gstamatakis95/lance-go/blob/main/lance/index.go#L110-L114>)

ZoneMap configures a ZoneMap index.

```go
type ZoneMap struct {
    // RowsPerZone is the number of rows per zone (0 = Lance default).
    RowsPerZone uint64
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```
